2 open spots
Everyone can ask questions
I've prepared some images that I'll send on whatsapp...

We are beginning the study of the message of Quran InshaAllah...


Allah asks us a few questions in the Quran:
أَم خُلق من غیر شئ ؟
Do you think you've come into existence by an accident? Just by yourself, Without a creator?

An ancient belief has been coming down through the ages....  that everything just came into existence all by itself... There is this universal matter.... this organic soup... that kept boiling and boiling and then one day the frog just jumped out...... The universe split open from a singulariy... and everything started forming all by itself....  the sun started getting hotter and hotter... the moon kept getting cooler and cooler... the winds started blowing... mountains started forming, the earth started rotating.... oceans started flowing... flowers started blooming... plants started growing....
So Allah asks us in the Quran...... What do you think? All of this happened just by itself?

Allama Iqbal... a famous poet of Pakistan says...



It is clearly impossible for things to get created all by themselves
If steel remains in one place, even for hundres of years... nobody has ever seen it turn into daggers and swords all by itself?
If you want to learn how to make a particular type of candy, you need a confectioner to teach you how to make it...

Raw material needs to be crafted into different forms.... skills need to be learnt.... nothing in this universe can happen by itself....

So Allah asks us...
أَم خُلق من غیر شئ ؟
Do you really think you've been created just like that by accident? Then he asks...
أَم ھُمُ الخالقون ؟
Have YOU created yourself? The color of your eyes... your facial features, you height, your skin tone, the family you were born into, the country you were born in.... did YOU choose all of this?
And then a third question...
أَم خُلِق السموات والارض
OK... forget about yourself for a second... What about these heavens and the earth? These lofty skies over your head that provide your means of sustenance and make life possible for you ... and what about this vast expanse under you feet... that you explore and walk on.... did YOU create all of this?

If you HAVE created all of this... if you have created yourself and everything around you.... then do as you please.... Nobody is going to question you about anything... Do good deeds, commit sins... construct mosques... build temples.... eat halal or drink alcohol... worship stones... do whatever you desire .... You are completely free to do as you please... Just prove that you are the creator of everything .... and then do whatever you desire... nobody is going to question you about anything....

But if you cannot prove that you've created all of this... then before you learn anything else.... before you become a doctor, before you beome an engineer, a lawyer, a psychologist, mathematician, computer engineer..... before everything else, the very first thing you need to know... the very first thing you need to learn.... is who created all of this that you see around you, and who did He create it for? Who created us? And Why did he create us? what does he want from you?

Syedna Jaffar bin Syedna Suleman QR says in his poems...



The eman, the faith in a believers heart is not complete until he understands 4 things
Where did man come from? and where will he return to?
And why was he sent in this world.... and why is he returning back?

So even though rheotrical.... these are extremely important quesitons that Allah challenges us with... Have we only been created to eat, drink, have children and then die? If that's the case... then even animals do that... so then what is the difference between us human beings and animals? Are we meant to live the lives of animals, or is there a greater purpose?


To answer this question, lets think about the one primary difference between us human beings and animals.... Did Allah give us something that he did not give to the animals? He did.... He blessed us with an intellect, العَقْلُ.... Al-Aql in Ancient Arabic is used for 2 things... First, Aql is used for a container that can hold something ..... and secondly Aql is used for a restraint.... the rope that is used to tie a camel is called an عِقَال....

Let's start with the first one... which is a container that can hold something.... we'll discuss the other one at a later time InshaAllah...

So linguistically speaking, Aql basically means a container... right? But what's really interesting is that in Arabic it signifies a very flexible container... A container that can expands to fit its contents.... The more contents you put in it, it'll stretch and expand to fit it... In other words, a container that can expand as its content increases.... You understand?  In contrast, a container that is fixed..... that does not expand, is called حاویۃ، أِناءَ، صندوق . These are containers that can hold only a fixed amount ... but not Al-Aql... The more you store into AlAql, it will expand to accomodate its contents.... Is that clear?

So then what is Aql a container for? What does it hold?
It's a container for العِلْمُ... knowledge! The more your knowledge expands, the more your intellect will expand with it.... to accomodate it.... And this is what we see in a child... as he grows older.... as he keeps assimilating knowledge, his intellect keeps growing... SubhanAllah!

Now what is Ilm or knowledge? Ilm in Arabic means the true quality of something... or the true reality of something... حقیقۃ الاشیاء..... And the faculty that can not only store... but also measure and differenciate between the quality of things is called Aql or intellect.... So two things... There is Ilm (knowledge) which is the true reality of something, and then there is the container for that knowledge that can store it, understand it, apply logic and reasoning to it... which is Al-Aql or intellect...
So gold is valuable metal .. that is a reality... a rock is worthless that is also a reality.... Intellect can not only store these realities as knowledge... but can also compare and differenciate between the two... It can understand that gold is more valuable than rocks... And as knowledge grows... intellect will also expand to accomodate more of it...

Any questions?


So Allah gave us this priceless gift of intellect... In fact it was the very first thing that he created.... There is this beautiful, and extremely profound Hadees of Rasul Allah (SAS) where he said....

اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل، فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل، و قال لہ ادبر فادبر
فقال لہ اللہ تبارک و تعاليٰ:  و عزتي و جلالي ما خلقت خلقاً اعظم منک ولا اَطوعُ منک
بک أُبدي و أُعيد، لک الثواب و عليک العقاب

The first thing that Allah created was the intellect.... And then he Commanded it to come forward! so it came forward... He then commanded it to move back! so it moved backwards.... Now what does it mean to move forward and to move backward? Freedom of Choice! Allah gave the intellect complete freedom of choice..... to do good, or to do bad... to accept or to refuse.... to obey or disobey.... to eat halaal or eat haraam... to submit or resist.... Allah gave the intellect complete freedom of choice.... فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل، و قال لہ ادبر فادبر

فقال لہ اللہ تبارک و تعاليٰ:  و عزتي و جلالي ما خَلَقْتَ خلقاً أعظمُ منک ولا أَطوعُ منک
Then Allah said to it... I swear by my strength and my power! I have not created anything greater than you.. ولا اَطوعُ منک nor have I created anything more obedient than you!
 بک أُبدي و أُعيد I am beginning creation through you, and through you that creation will return to me...  لک الثواب و عليک العقاب  Because of your good decisions they will be rewarded and because of your bad decisions they will be punished... SubhanAllah!


And then Rasul Allah (SAS) said...

اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل ۔ فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل و قال لہ ادبر فادبر
فقال لہ اللہ تبارک و تعاليٰ:  و عزتي و جلالي ما خلقت خلقاً اعظم منک ولا اَطوعُ منک
بک أُبدي و أُعيد، لک الثواب و عليک العقاب ۔
تشعب من العقل الحلم ، و من الحلم العلم، و من العلم الرشد، و من الرشد العفاف
و من العفاف الصيانة، و من الصيانة الحيائ، ومن الحياء الرَّزانة
  من الرَّزانة المَداَوِمَةِ عليٰ الخير ، و کراھية الشر، و من کراھية الشر طاعة الناصح
فھذہ عشرة اصناف من انواع الخير، و لکل واحد من ھذہ العشرة الاصناف، عشرة انواع
تشعب من العقل الحلم from the intellect, forebearance was created
و من الحلم العلم and forebearance created knowledge
و من العلم الرشد and knowledge created guidance
و من الرشد العفاف and guidance created virtue
 و من العفاف الصيانة and virtue created safeguard
و من الصيانة الحيائ  and safeguard created shame
ومن الحياء الرزانة and shame created dignity

و من الرزانة المداومة عليٰ الخير و کراھية الشر، and dignity created the love for good and disgust for evil
و من کراھية الشر طاعة الناصح and the one who was disgusted by evil, became obedient to the counselor

فھذہ عشرة اصناف من انواع الخير، و لکل واحد من ھذہ العشرة الاصناف عشرة انواع These are 10 characteristics from the categories of good, and for every one of these 10 characteristics, there are 10 further characteristics....


So through the creation of intellect, these 10 primary qualities were created of forebearance, knowledge, guidance, virtue, protection, shame, dignity, attraction towards good and repulsion from evil, and this distinction between good and evil let the intellect to become obedient to the one giving counsel.... So the final purpose... the fruit of intellect is obedience... Obedience to who? To the one giving counsel.... to the one providing guidance....... the message and the messenger! Obedience to Quran and Rasul Allah (SAS)

So obedience is the purpose of intellect!

Any Questions?

But we know that even animals have some lower form of intellect... because even they can differentiate between good and bad.... If you put stale dried up grass before a goat... and then at a short distance, you put some fresh grass..... it'll ignore the dry grass and eat the fresh grass... because it can distinguish between good and bad.... You put raw meat, as well as cooked meat in front of a lion, it will eat the raw meat... even though both are meat... So animals too can differentiate


So then what's the difference in intellect between us and them? Imam Jaffar AlSadiq (AS) defined human intellect as a faculty that can not only distinguish between good and bad, but it can also distinguish between two goods and two bads.... I'll say that again...  X2
When faced with two goods, it can choose the greater good and when faced with 2 evils, it can choose the lesser evil.... And based on this separation... this ability to choose between 2 goods and 2 bads... man has made all the progress we see in the world today! This is something only human beings can do... animals cannot do that... That's why Intellect is THE greatest gift that Allah has bestowed on His creation... for the purpose of obedience


Lastly, Aql also means a restraint.... Its a rope used to tie a camel.... عقل البعیر literally means tying the camel...... and the rope used to tie it is called an عِقَال.  So intellect is also a restraint... restraint for what? For the soul! What happens when the camel is tied to a post? Its movement gets limited.... I can now only move within the boundary permitted by the length of the rope...

So intellect is a restraint for the soul.... so that it can establish a set boundary for itself and move or operate only within that boundary... The boundary of whats permissible... Everything within the boundary is permissible and everything outside of it is not permissable... The soul can restrain itself within this boundary using the intellect...
 

None of what we've discussed so far has anything to do with Islam yet.... This is not something specific to the Muslims... this is applicable to all human beings in general.. So what have we learnt so far about the intellect so far?

It's a container that holds knowledge, which expands as knowledge increases
It was the first thing that Allah created, and gave it complete freedom of choice
It was created for the purpose of obedience
It is a scale, a meezaan, that can differentiate between 2 goods and 2 evils.
It is also a restraint, a boundary of what's acceptable for the soul... The soul can use it to stop itself from committing

Now actually let's look at this in action... Let's do an exercise together...

Going to the movie vs Namaaz -------  Both are good ------ which one do you think I will choose? Why?
Movie is good for 2 reasons - (1) its reward is visible (2) it's immediately accessible ... it's close.... I can enjoy it right now.
Namaaz on the other hand.. (1) reward is not visible... because I will not see it until I die (2) It is not immediately accessible... I have to wait 50 70 years before I can access it...

Therefore I choose what?


So you see the human intellect will always choose what it believes is beneficial for its soul.... To the intellect visible and immediately accessible is always better than invisible and distant... That's why it is so easy to choose this world over the afterlife.... Duniya over Aakhira... That's what the word duniya actually means in classical Arabic... It comes from the word دنی  which means something that is close and it also means something that is inferior أدنی. ... This is why the life in this world is called حیاۃ الدنیا . The inferior life... We think it means the life of this world... but linguistically speaking it actually means the inferior life! Why? Because if there is an inferior life, just by contrast there has to be what? A superior life حیاۃ الآخرۃ. The word آخرۃ comes from the word خیر  which means good or superior... When you add a Ta Marboota at the end of it, as in the word آخرۃ it means best of the best! SubhanAllah! You see how beautiful the Arabic language is?

So anything in duniya is low and inferior, however... it is always what? visible and readily accessible! And to the intellect, visible and readily accessible is a good deal!

.
people find beauty in the love of that which they desire - of women, and sons, mountains of gold and silver, fine branded horses and cattle and tilled land... That is the enjoyment of this worldly (inferior) life... but what Allah has with Him is the best of returns
 

So what then can change our perspective?  What can switch our perspective to see Akhirah as visible and readily accessible? What can make us see the akhira as gold and this world as rocks? What do we need to do in order to change our perspective? We need to study the Quran! It is only through the study of this book that we will see what this world in reality is and how superior the life of the hereafter is! Only this book can help us keep our eyes on the prize as they say!


This is no different than your school life... The intelligent one... the one of intellect... focuses on his education and delays his desire for fun and enjoyment till after he graduates... he keeps his eyes on the prize... on that degree that will earn him a good life... On the other hand the one who is focused on instant gratification, enjoys his way through school with fun and games and partying and alcohol and drugs.. but what happens when it's time to graduate? He has nothing... he spends the remainder of his life flipping burgers at McDonalds...

The life and pleasures of this world are definitely accessible... they're right in front of you.. all you have to do is stretch your arm forward and grab it... but its pleasures are short... it is temporary, it's inferior, it's filled with losses and pain and miseries.. It's "Duniya" from the word "Danaa" which means inferior.... But the pleasures of the afterlife are never ending.. they are permanent... only happiness, only pleasure.. never ending happiness... ever growing pleasures.... It is "Akhirah" from the word "Khair" meaning the best of the best!

That's why Maulana Ali (AS) in his final words... after he was struck by the sword of Ibn Muljim AlLaeen---- gives advise to his beloved sons Imam Hassan and Hussain... and explains to them the inferiority and imperfection of this worldly life as his final words...
أَحیَ قَلبَکَ بِالمَوعِضَۃ، وَ نَوِّرہُ بِالحِکمَۃ، وَقوِّھِی بِالزُّھَد، وَذَلِّلہُ بِالمَوت، وَقَرِّرہُ بِالفَناَء،وحَزِّنہُ وَسُولَتَ الدَّھَر،وَتَقَلُّبَ الَّیالِی وَالأَیَّام ،  وأَعرِض علیهِ أخبارالماضین وذکرهُ بما أصابَ مَن کانَ قَبلُکَ مِن الاوَّلِین ۔ و سِر فِی دِیارِهِم فانْظُر فی آثارِهِم ۔مَا فَعَلُو؟ و أَینَ حَلُّو ؟ فأِنَّک تَریٰ قَد أَنتَقِلُو مِن دارِ الغُرور وَنَزلُو فی دَارِ الغُربۃ۔  فأِنَّ سِرتَک أحدِھِم۔
ولا تبع آخرتک بالدنیا ، بل بیع دنیا بآخرتک

Even if you don't understand what I just said... you can still enjoy the perfection and the beauty of the style of his words ...... but what I actually wanted to share with you were these words....
ولا تبع آخرتک بالدنیا ، بل بیع دنیا بآخرتک
My sons, soon a day will come ... when on one hand there will be this inferior world..... and on the other hand you will have the afterlife... And you will find yourself in a dilemma.... if you try to save the life of this world, you'll end up lose the after life... and if you try to save your after life you'll lose your life in this world.... When that day comes..... save your after life... and let go of the pleasures of this inferior world
ولا تبع آخرتک بالدنیا ، بل بیع دنیا بآخرتک

If you have to let go of this world, then let it go...... because death will eventually snatch it away from you anyway... But never let go of your after life.... never let go of your afterlife...
By the pains of drinking poison and their own behedding, and the behedding of their family members in karbala, these last words of their father are being honored....
 






















اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل

and then did what? He tested it to make sure it is functioning properly...
فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل، و قال لہ ادبر فادبر



why do you think we do bad deeds?





أَم خُلق من غیر شئ ؟
Were you just created by accident?
أَم ھُمُ الخالقون ؟
Have you created yourselves?
أَم خُلِق السموات والارض
Have you created the heavens and the earth?










So that we can think! So that we can reflect and examine His signs in the universe so that we can recognize His existence.... so that we could conclude that this magnificent universe around us, could not have come into existence by itself.... and assuming for a second, that it did come into existence by itself.... how is it being maintained for billions of years without someone maintaing it? If this is all happening all by itself, by it's own free volition, then why doesn't the night preceed the day? Why doesn't fall come immediately after spring? Why does water always fall from the sky? why does the earth's rotation never vary in speed? Why are the planets so consistently and uniformly moving within their orbits? Why don't they ever step out?
How can all this order exist in the universe without someone forcing this order on to them?  A child's tricycle cannot ride itself, the child has to ride it.... this huge magnificent universe is functioning so perfectly all by itself? how is this notion comprehensible... even conceivable? It's logically impossible! So there has to be creator who created you, me and everything around us.... there has to be a maintainer, a sustainer who is controlling and maintaining everything around us... if the planets are bound in an orbit... then logically there has to be someone who has bound them in their orbits... Even if its gravity... there has to be someone who created gravity.. someone who is not allowing the value of that gravity to change...



 









There are these allegations against Islam that it is a very difficult religion.... I want to tell you today that this allegation is not true... Islam is not a difficult religion... it is  a very very difficult ... I'm kidding...

Islam is a religion of nature... It is called Deen Al-Fitrah... which means the religion of nature... InshaAllah as we progress, you'll see that everything about Islam is based on natural phenomemon... It conforms to the nature of human being.... And the nature of human beings conforms to the nature of Allah Himself... Allah says

فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًۭا ۚ فِطْرَتَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى فَطَرَ ٱلنَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا ۚ لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَلِكَ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْقَيِّمُ وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
Turn your face toward the religion of truth. The nature of Allah upon which He has created [all] people. No change should there be in the creation of Allah. That is the straight religion, but most of the people do not know.

فِطْرَتَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِى فَطَرَ ٱلنَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا
The nature of Allah is the same on which he molded the nature of mankind.... InshaAllah we'll discuss the depth of this ayah at a later time... but I want to highlight from this ayah is that Allah created human beings according to his own nature... And then gave them a religion which conforms to his nature...

Anything that is natural... human being will find it easy to accept... He will be comfortable with it... Anything that is unnatural.. human beings will fear and run away from it... This is presented with a beautiful example...
Movement is associated with life... it is natural for the living to move... As long as you see a living person making some sort of movement... you remain comfortable... Isn't that true? If you see a loved one sleeping, but him making some movement.. like breathing ... you remain content... but if you see that loved one not moving... you get scared... You go and shake him to make sure he's still alive.... Why is that? Because movement is natural for a living being... If the living doesn't move it scares us....
Similarly if the dead suddenly starts moving.. that scares us too! Why? Because the dead is not supposed to move!  It is not natural for the dead to move...

So Islam is a religion of nature.... Islam is actually a very simple religion... People overcomplicate the heck out of it.... but in reality , in its essence.... Islam is very simple... Because it is in accordance with the nature of human beings... And Quran is the book that contains the description of all those natural laws...  It's a book of guidance... you know how video games have walk throughs... You can purchase these books that tell you how you can get through each level of a game? Quran is that book that helps you walk through the game of life... It's really that simple!!! It really is!

And we actually see examples of this all around us!

Any company that makes a product... always provides an instruction manual for it... doesn't it? You buy a radio, it comes with an instruction manual; you buy a watch it comes with a user guide; you buy a laptop, a phone, a TV, they all come with a user guide... instructions on how to operate the device..... There is no product in the world that comes without a set of instructions....right? Well this universe....  and everything in it...... is Allah product... it is Allah's creation, and all of this too comes with a set of instructions..... an instruction manual called the Quran...

Now what is any instruction manual? It is nothing more than a set of dos and don'ts... correct? Congratulations on the purchase of your brand new iphone... begin using it by sliding the start button to the right... select an application by clicking on it.... press the round button at the bottom of the phone to return to the main screen.... press these buttons on the side to increase or decrease the volume....  Do NOT drop your phone from a height... do not use it as a door stopper.... do NOT try to hammer a nail into the wall with it.... if it gets dirty... do NOT wash it in the washing machine....  if you accidentally do, do NOT dry it in the dryer... If you do, and  the dryer makes loud rattling noises... do NOT restart the cycle again....  No! I never did any of that!

Similarly the Quran is a set of dos and don't instructions for Allahs product.... the human being... DO these things so that you can accomplish the following benefits... DO NOT do these things so that you can avoid the following harms they cause.... as simple as that...

Every company comes with a logo, that identifies the manufacturer of the product.... you see a half bitten apple you know the product was manufactured by Apple.... You see flying windows you know the product was manufactured by microsoft.... you see a funny looking lady in a green circle.. you know it was made by starbucks.... Allah's brand, his logo is His kalima shahada..... la ilaha illa allah.... You see a person reciting La Ilaha Illa Allah you know his heart belongs to Allah... You know his heart is powered by Allah!

Every product needs a demonstration... Millions of dollars are spent by compaines creating demonstration sites or videos, or confrences where someone demonstrates the use of the product.... Someone stands on stage and practically walks you through the use of the product... practically shows you... by example... how to perform each operation listed in the instruction manual... Yes??
Rasul Allah (SAS) is the living demonstration of the Quran.... He is the embodiment of every do and don't listed in the Quran.. He practically demonstrated for us what to do, how to do it, when to do it, and how much to do it.... He also demonstrated for us what not to do... how not to do it... when not to do it... Every single instruction of the Quran, you can find its practical form in the seerah of Rasul Allah... (SAS).

Every product also has a technical manual... that is not for everyone... it is only for the technically advanced people... It transcends beyond the superficial operations into the technical operations... How is this device structured... what is the schematic diagram.. how is it functioning internally.... How is a change in this part of the device affecting a change in the other parts of the diagram... Why is this device working the way it is... how has it been internally designed... all the technical specs....
Maulana Ali is the technical guide for Islam... All branches of knowledge... taveel, mabda maad, haqaiq, daqaiq, haqaiq Aliyah, daqaiq ul daqaiq, ifadaats... all of these highly technical realms of knowledge emerge from him and his progeny the Imams of all times... They explain the why of things... why namaaz, why roza... what is ruku, what is sujood.... what are the mamsoolat... how is the universe actually functioning.. how physicallity is affecting spirituality... how spirituality is having an impact on physicallity... All these highly technical details... the inner workings of this this universe... our existence... our return... all of this exists in the technical uloom of Maulana Ali and the line of Imams in his progeny....

So you see, this concept of religion is not such a far fetched idea... it's pretty straight forward... And the center of this circle is the Quran... And InshaAllah we will look at everything only from the Quran... because if we want to know about Allah... who better to ask than Allah himself right? Through the miracles of Quran sessions... we're already convinced InshaAllah that this message is in fact from Allah... Now let's actually start looking at the message itself...




We should really examine our own lives and the lives of those around us... Why is it that the desires of our heart never end? Some People spend all their time on facebook, whatsapp, all these social media sites... desperately trying to find a connection...  why does this heart constantly long for a connection? And no matter how hard we try... why can't we find that eternal connection... why do we always have that feeling of emptiness in our hearts no matter how hard we try to fill it? Some try to fill that emptiness with money, some with power, some try to drown it in alcolhol, some try to forget it with drugs.... but that emptiness is always there... If money is the key to all happiness then why are so many rich people committing suicides? It is understandable that if the poor keeps running after wealth, but why do we see the filthy rich running after money as well? The guy is a millionaire, but his heart still wants more? He wants to be billionarie... Why is that? Even with all the comforts of this world... why is this heart never satisfied? Why is it always looking for more?

Look at the artists in the music industry. The artists starts off by desiring for their song to be heard... He gets a break, someone produces his album... everybody downloads it... it goes platinum... they become extremely populare in one night... extremely wealthy as a result... He wins a music award! Now is that the end of his career? No! Now they have to come up with the next big hit.... but the next album they release is not as entertaining.... not as successful... not as many sales... didn't make it to the top of the charts... now the artist starts feeling desperate.... she starts seeing her own decline.... what does every artist want? Even  more than money? Fame! Appreciation, Acknowledgement, recognition! They live off of that... but instead of that now she's getting criticisms... rotten tomatos, one start, thumbs down.... she starts getting depressed... so she starts thinking what can i do to get back on top? I need to come up with a new song... wait! I already tried that... didn't work out too well... Maybe if my new song was a little controvertial... maybe if it was a little more indecent.. it might get more popular... does that happen?
And then when that doesn't work as well then maybe if I drop my standards of decency even further... maybe that'll get me there... If that doesn't happen... maybe I'll just shave my head... get drunk.. get arrested... adopt a baby... adopt 12 babies....Maybe if I can't be mentioned at the award ceremony.. maybe I'll be mentioned in the tabloid at the end of a grocery aisle. At least I'll be mentioned somewhere... Does that happen? Sure! They get more and more and more desperate... willing to try anything... when nothing works, they switch to alcolhol, then drugs and then one day they're found dead in the bathtub of their penthouse suite....

And most importantly, what does this "need for" more indicate?  It indicates that the heart is not getting what it truly needs....
Let's say you have some critical data on your phone that you need for work or school... You don't have a backup of it... the only original copy you have of it is on your phone.... That data is so important, that if you don't have it you can't function at work or school....  Now what happends when you lose your phone with that data on it? You will search for it right? you will keep searching for it everywhere.... in your house... in your office... the places you visited... you will keep searching endlessly for it....
Now I can distract you temporarily... take your mind off it for a bit.... right? I can distract you with facebook, whatsapp, hanging out with friends, PS4, Xbox etc....  but the moment the distraction is over... what do you do? You start searching for you phone again right? Now there is only one way in which this need... this search can end... What is that? It is If you find your phone.... Once you find it, will you search for it anymore? Of course not!

You see... the problem is that all these worldly luxuries can provide comfort to our bodies.... but not to our hearts... Because the heart was never meant for this world... And we constatnly try to satisfy its needs with worldly posessions... money, fame, luxuries, all sorts of jewellery and bling... But its need is not physical in nature.... The body's need is physical.... The hearts need is spiritual.... and until it finds the object of its search, the heart will always remain dissatisifed...

So then what is the heart looking for? And how can it achieve the satisfaction it desires? Well Allah answers these questions very plainly in the Quran...
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Beware! Only with by the rememberance of Allah, your heart will finds peace...

Allah is the ultimate goal of the hearts... Until it finds Allah, the heart does not find peace... Like a fish out of water, yearns to return back to it... our hearts crave for the love of Allah.... Like a man thirsty for water is not satisfied with anything else.... he is not distracted by anything except water... even if you give him the best juices, the most delicious drinks.... he still craves for that cold sip of water that can extinguish his thirst completely.... hearts cannot find satisfaction until it finds Allah!

So Allah asks all of us
أَم خُلق من غیر شئ ؟
Have you come into existence all by yourself?
أَم ھُمُ الخالقون ؟
Have YOU ou created yourself?
أَم خُلِق السموات والارض
Have you created the heavens and the earths?

If not, then the first responsibility I have as his creation, is that I find out who my creator is? And then find out why has he created me and what is it that he wants from me? Why did he create this world? Why did he create me and everyone in it? Then what is this thing called death? Why do we die? Even though I don't want to die... Who ever wishes to die? Who would desire to leave this huge setup that one spends his entire life building from the ground up... So then who is killing us? Who is removing us without our will from our thrones and putting us under the ground? What happens after death? Where do we go? What's going to happen when we get there? Who are these prophets? Why have they been sent? and Who sent them?

These are extremely important questions that we need to ask ourselves... these are extremely important facts that we need to understand.... And the best place to find these answers is in the book sent down BY the creator FOR His creation, which is the Quran..... This book, the Quran, holds answers to all our questions...


أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
don't you see they wander aimlessly in every valley?

ھاما فی کل واد. hama is used for a camel when it gets lost in the desert looking for water... It goes in one valley, then another valley, then another valley.. searching for water.. aimlessly waandering looking for water... this is how Allah describes those wandering trying to find a connection elsewhere....

do artists have power? more than they realize... they have the power to affect 1000s upon 1000s of minds... what you're listening to is affecting your thoughts... and your thoughts are affecting your behavior and your speech... the stuff you take in... will be the stuff that comes out
کُلُّ أِناَءٍ بِمَا فِیہِ یَنضَا
If we constantly take in filth... what's going to come out of us? the same filth... that's what'' heppen... what's going to go in is eventually going to come out...

 

















































The Quran gives us roads to conviction.... How are you absolutely convinced that this is the truth? This is revelation from Allah? First step... is there in fact a God? there is in fact an Ilah... How do you get to that absolute conviction that this is the case.... That its not a theory.. not a theology.. it is an aboslute fact...

The qurans argument is that if people genuinely reflect upon the creations around them... there is no way they will reach any other conclusion! That is the Quran's argument

إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَآيَتٍۢ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَبِ
Verily, In the heavens and the earth, and the changing of night and day, there are miraculous signs for those of intellect

What is an ayah? What does a sign mean? Relation of sign with intellect

In other words Allah did not even use revelation to prove His existance.... Allah did not use Quran to prove his existence... the proof of existence of Allah is not in the Quran interestingly enough... Allah never talks about proof of his existence in the Quran. Its not even a subject... If an athiest asks.. show me a surah in which Allah proves he exists... we can't do that...  I don't have one.

What doubt does Quran address? Whether this is revelation or not!
ذلک الکتاب لا ریب فیہ
no where in the Quran does Allah say
ذلک الألہ لا ریب فیہ
Its not a discussion in the Quran. Because Allahs declration in the qauran is that you already have another set of ayats... another set of signs that will bring you to that conclusion.... You don't need thise ayats in my book for that..... look around you .. look at all the signs i've placed all around you .... those will automatically bring you to that conclusion. What are those signs? The skies the earth, the mountains, the clouds, the breeze, the rain, the plants, the animals... all of this will bring you to that conclusion if you seriously and honestly reflect ..... it'll become very hard for you to say that this is a product of an accident...  Nobody in their right mind will be able to say that all of this is a part of an accident.... People cannot  explain simple things like beauty! Why would beauty exists as part of an accident? Why would delicious taste exist as part of an accident?  why would that exist? How would that make any sense?
How would symmetry exist in the universe as a part of an accident? a little snow flake... why does it have incredibly artistic symmetry? we have Super expensive chandelliers designed inspired by that? How could that just be because of an accident? it should have chaos... it should be all mangled and twisted... why is it so beautiful? SubhanAllah! Look at the symmetry in a leaf...  just examine it.... how the left side mirrors the right side! How could this be an accident?

You know how you walk into the store and pickup the iphone 6 or ths samsung galaxy and admire it... Hmm so sleek, so beautiful... amazing symmetry.... who's the designer?  Who's the manufacturer?  That leaf is Allah's design... he is the manufacturer of that leaf... look at it! appreciate it.... it give you something else...

Even with the phones... Did the iphone come together as a result of an accident? did the flip phones over time just by themselves, by some cosmic accident...  evolve into the iphone 6? No... somebody designed the flip phone and somebody designed the iphone... 
Now If you can't believe that a simple device like a phone couldn't come together by some accident... ask yourself this as relfect on this huge universe.. did these entire universes came into existence by an accident?

Seriously... If you ever have a crisis of faith... simply step out of your home and examine nature around you... reflect upon it...  because for a muslim there is no greater exercise in increasing Eman, other than just reflecting on nature... SubhanAllah! You can talk to Allah througha  flower.... You can talk to Allah through the bark of a tree.... just the breeze you feel on your face... reminds you of Allah!  This is the true Muslim life..


You don't really ahve to be sitting in these sunday sessions or any islamic lecture to experience faith... you just have to pay attention to the world around you... You just have to pay attention to the ayaats Allah create for you all around you...

This is so beautiful... Such amazing symmetry.. the verses in the Quran are called ayaats.... These ayats of the Quran push you outside to examine the ayaats outside of the Quran... and the ayaats that are outside.. push you back to the Quran to understand the one the signs are pointing to! SubhanAllah.... The book forces us to look at the world... and the world forces us back to look into the book... Quran as the center of the circle... pushes us out in the universe ... all the way to the circumfrence of the circle and everything inside the circle pushes us back to the center.... SubhanAllah.. How amazing is that? We're constantly bouncing between the book and the universe... our entire life is in going from one set of ayats to another set of ayats ... back and forth....

Allah is going to say to the disbelievers on judgement day... what you didn't feel the wind? the soft breeze on your face? 
فلملکیات ذکرا۔ عذرا و نظرا
Allah says the winds are my reminder... you and i think that these sessions, these majalis, these asbaaq are our reminders... Allah says in the Quran that the wind is enough as my reminder.... عذرا اور نظرا. The wind will be enought for them to say I am willing to change master... i recognize and submit to your power.... like the wind was slap on their face for them to get their act together.... They experience faith just by experiencing teh breeze on their face... WOW!!



And the one who becomes concious of thes internal and external ayats... what happens to him?
فزادتھم ایمانٌ
he experiences an increase in faith.... his faith increaess ... his faith becomes strong...


























 


سَنُرِيهِمْ آيَتِنَا فِى ٱلأٓفَاقِ وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَتَّىٰ يَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِ بِرَبِّكَ أَنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ شَهِيدٌ

In the near future We show them.... in horizons and their souls

This whole imagery is of desert life

أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِهَدًۭا
didn't we make the earth into a mihaad? مھاد comes from the word مھد. which means a cradle. When a mother holds her baby in her arms it's called a مھد. comforting it... neck is supported... back is not bent over. she cradles the child perfectly for the comfort of her child.. Allah says I made the entire world a mihaad for you... The most beautiful image of sleep... is the image of a child isn't it? Allah says I made the entire world as comfortable as a mothers cradle for you... what bedding will you compete with?

وَٱلْجِبَالَ أَوْتَادًۭا
and the mountains as pegs...

So the mother cradles the baby and then walks into her tent... What holds the tent together? The pegs! the اوتاد. Allah says look at your pegs and compare them to my tents.. And by the way tents are shaped like what? Mountains! Allah says look at your tents and look at my tents... This is what you made... this is what I made... I pegged mountains on this earth so that you could be in awe!! That this is how we construct and this is how Allah constructs

Who do you think you are?
 


Going to do with entire session with you..

In the previous session we started talking about the meaning of the word intellect العقل .... I presented to you the hadees of Rasul Allah (SAS) about the intellect... Today I'd like to explore the same concepts in some more detial... So what did we say the word Aql means....

The first meaning of the word العقل , as I explained..... is that of a container..... Now when I explain the meaning of an Arabic word to you... you have to always keep in mind..... that I'm not talking about spoken Arabic.... I'm not talking about Egyptian, Morrocoon, Kuwaiti or Saudi Arabic.... The Arabic we hear today all around us .... is spoken Arabic... It is distorted, morphed, disgusting Arabic... You hear two Arabs speaking today and it can actually damage your ears... Hammana Hammana Hammana.. That's all you hear....
But then you hear things like Arabic is such a beautiful... such an eloquent language.... right??  So you need to have this distinction, that when someone refers to the beauty of Arabic, especially in the context of Quran.... he is not referring to spoken Arabic... he is not even referring to proper Arabic... he's referring to what? Ancient Arabic!
Quran was not revealed for the Kuwaitis or the Saudis or the Egyptians... It was revealed to the Ancient Arabs... So in order to understand the depth in the meanings of this book... we have to understand it from the mindset of the Ancient Arab.... not todays Arab! So if you look up the word Aql in an arabic to English dictionary today ... you'll find it translated as just Intellect.... that's it! Because that's all it means to the Arabs today... but that's not what it meant to the Arabs during the time of Rasul Allah (SAS).... And that's exactly what we're interested in learning.... That what did the word عقل mean to the ancient Arabs for whom the Quran was revealed.... So you always need to keep this distinction in mind.... not just for what I say today.... but also for all future sessions....

So we discussed that العقل is a flexible container for علم or knowledge, that expands as knowledge increases...
And as we learnt from Rasul Allah (SAS) Hadees.... that it was the first thing that Allah created.... it was the BEST and the loftiest thing that Allah created...and after its creation, Allah gave it complete freedom of choice....
اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل، فقال لہ اقبل فاقبل، و قال لہ ادبر فادبر
Let's explore this hadees a bit further... linquistically...

The first thing we notice is that the hadees states that it was the very first thing that Allah created... اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل Now why is that important? If you have a lot of tasks that you need to complete for school.... what do you do? You prioritize them, right? What are the things you take into consideration when setting your priorities? Urgency and Importance could be one of them... length of the tasks in comparison to the available time... But the first thing you do, is what? create the task list itself before you start actually working on the tasks.... You have to write 6 different papers for your school.... You have all the reference books stacked up on your table... you have a list of all the links to the sites that you're going to need... You have your priority set in terms of which paper you will write first... You have your supply of cookies and your cadburry eggs next to you.... you've chosen the playlist of the tunes you're going to play as you work on these papers... Awesome... Time to being... Time to write the first paper... what do you need? A pen! If you don't have a pen... does anything else matter?? .... except the cadburry eggs of course? No! It's the very first thing you need in order for you to write you papers....

So Allah has created the heaves and the earths and everything that exists in it... He's created these mansions upon mansions in heaven, and he's created the hell.... he's planned out his messengers... I'm going to send Adam first, then Nooh, then Ibrahim, then Moosa, then Eisa (علیھم السلام). And then finally I'm going to send my beloved prophet Muhammad (SAS) with this extremely elaborate and extremely eloquent and symmetrically miraculous message for my creations so that they can understand it and earn my reward and get closer to me and my love... oh wait... what is the first thing they need in order to do that? The intellect! Without the intellect nothing else is going to matter! without the intellect he's not going to recognize my messenger, nor his message... He will not be able to see the miracles in the message that I've placed for him...and without the message he will not be able to come close to me.... he will not be able to get into this beautiful paradise that I've created for him...

Therefore what was the first thing that Allah created? The intellect! SubhanAllah!

Now if intellect is the first thing Allah created.... what is the first thing we should be using to understand his religion? The intellect! So now you tell me... how could I have agreed to responses like don't use your intellect... don't think too much about religion... it'll drive you nuts.... things like that? How could I have accepted that? Before any decision we make in our lives... how can we not present it before the intellect first? Is this going to benefit me in the long run? Or is it going to hurt me in the long run? Do I need this instant pleasure right before me? Or do I want the lasting eternal pleasure that is to come....




The word اقبل comes from the root word قِبل, which means to face something or someone... From that same root word we get the word Qibla... as in Ka'aba is our qibla.... why? Because we turn our face towards it in prayer....  أقبل is an imperative verb.... it's used when giving a command..... a command to someone to either face them.... or step forward facing towards them...... So If I say for example:
أقبَلَ علیہ بالسَّیف it means I'm commanding someone... Advance towards him with a sword!!!!
ادبر on the other hand, means to walk away from somone.... with their back turned towards them... So if I tell you to step forward, you can move towards me, while facing me... and if said move back, you could also move back while still facing me... but that would not be ادبر ... The word ادبر suggests, to actually turn around and walk away from me.... showing me your back... So when Allah commanded the intellect to move towards him by saying أقبل ... it walked towards Him... and when He commanded it to move back by saying ادبر .... it literally turned around and walked away from Allah, showing Allah its back... And what does it mean to turn your back? It means to either refuse something, to stop being involved in something, to reject something. So what the hadees states is that when Allah created this superior entity that He called the intellect... He created it with the potential to accept the truth.... but also to turn away from it.... It had the capability to do both... It could walk towards Allah... it could also turn its back on Allah and walk away from Allah.... You understand?

Other narrations extend this hadees further to say.... that after commanding the intellect to move forward and move backward... Allah also said
ثم قال لہ اَقعُدْ فَقَعَدَ ثم قال لہ قُم فَقَام
After that Allah ordered the intellect to sit down and so it sat down... then Allah ordered it to rise ۔۔ and so it stood up... Meaning what? Become passive or become active....  So this hadees suggests 4 possibilities of the intellect....
First to move towards Allah.... second to turn around and move away from Allah.... third is to sit down, which means to compromise for the greater good like what Imam Hassan (AS) did. He compromised with Mua'wiyah for the protection of Islam.... and the fourth to stand its ground no matter what, as in what Imam Hussain (AS) did in Karbala against Yazeed, also for the protection of Islam....

So after giving the intellect these 4 potentials Allah informed the intellect about its importance... and the role it is going to play in the lives of those who will receive this gift.... Allah said...
و عزتي و جلالي! ما خلقت خلقاً أعظمُ منک ولا اَطوعُ منک
I swear by my honor and superiority! I have not created anything more superior or obedient than you!
The first thing to notice here is that Allah out of His 99 names.... chose two particular names to swear by... عزتي و جلالي By my honor and my superiority... Now the question is why did he choose these 2 names in particular... It's because this intellect that he created..... was a representative of these two names... Honor and Superiority!
1. A man is honored and dignified in this world and the hereafter by his intellect.... and the arabic word عزۃ represents honor and dignity.... that's why he swore by عزتي . 
2. And the intellect is also the most superior thing that Allah created as he himself declared: ما خلقت خلقاً اعظم منک  I have not created created anything more suprior than you.... and this supriority is from his جلالۃ. That's why he swore by his superiority... و جلالي
Then he said: ولا اَطوعُ منک I have not created anything more obedient than you.. Now let's understand this word obedience becuase it can have a negative connotation to it. The word obedience, especially in the context of Islam means: willfull compliance with an order, request, or law. It also means willing submission to an authority. Now this is really important to understand.... because obedience can also be achieved unwillingly... Obedience can be forced through fear... You can scare someone into obedience ... and it can also be doctored through hope of a reward... for example, I can say if you obey what I'm asking you to do, then I'll give you something in return as your reward....
InshaAllah we'll cover the topic of obedience in detail later on when we talk about love..... For now I just want you to understand.... that the obedience Allah talks about in this hadees.... is the obedience that stems from pure love.... It is WILLING submission of the soul to the command of Allah.... purely out of deep rooted love for him... because of accepting him as an الہ.... not because of fear or hope...

So Allah informed the intellect that obedience or submission is part of your nature.... because I have not created anything more obedient than you... ولا اَطوعُ منک. The only quesiton remains... is who will you be obedient to? Who will you surrender yourself to? Will you be obedient to My message and My messenger? Or will you be obedient to Iblees and his messengers? That.... I will leave up to you... But know that you will definitely submit....  You will either return to me as my obedient followers ; or you will return to me as Shaitaans slaves... What you choose is up to you...
بک أُعبَدُ و بک أُعصیٰ
You can choose to worship me, or you can chose to disobey me... Then He said
بک أُبدي و أُعيد، و لک الثواب و عليک العقاب
I am beginning my creation with you... making you their guide... giving you the capability to accept truth or reject it..... compromise with your desires... or stand firmly on your belief..... and I'm granting you complete freedom of choice.... But know that based on how you guide the souls.... they will either receive my rewards.... or they will incur my wrath... Bascially YOU will be responsible for their success and you will be responsible for their desctruction....

Does everyone understand everything so far?

Now there are a couple of questions that arise at this point.... I'll state the questions first and then let's discuss them individually...

1. Why did Allah give the intellect complete freedom? Isn't that the root of all problems? We make bad choices because we CAN make bad choices.... Allah could have just restricted our intellects to do good..... couldn't He? He could have programmed it only to follow good and turn away from evil....  So then why didn't he??? Wouldn't that have been perfect for us?
Program is the definition of a machine. It would have created a slave mentality. Allah wants us to come to Him with love not by force
To distinguish the pious from the wretched
Example of microwave

2. He says all reward and punishment will depend on the choices that the intellect makes...  What is this reward He's referring to? Jannah right?  And what is the punishment he's talking about? Hell! So why not just create everyone in Jannah? After all he did create Jannah for us human beings only right? So why didn't he just do that? Why did he associate this reward and punishment with the intellect?
Justice to all creation
Reward is defined as: something given in recognition of one's service, effort, or achievement.
And if Jannah is a reward, then by definition of the word reward... it has to be earned...
Jannah is a place of ranks - ranks demand effort and achievements.... and achievements depend on intellect...
In a school system.. you have grades based on how students perform... but babies in nurseries don't have ranks... they're all equal.... A nursery teacher never says anything like you're baby just topped among all babies... because topped in what? Maybe pooping in diapers? There is no intellect at that level... therefore... no effort, no achievements to be graded on...
And Justice dictacates that reward has to be based on achievement

Why not declare all students in a class as toppers?
Why not pay the doctors just as much as the clerks?
In a company why not have all employees get an equal salary?


So what have we discussed so far about the intellect?
1. It is a container for knowledge that expands as the knowledge increases
2. It was the first thing that Allah created... It was the loftiest.... the most superior creation of Allah..... created for the purpose of obedience (willing submission to Allah). It was created with the potential to obey or disobey with complete freedom of choice... And this is the stance that Islam takes in its message... the stance of choice!

وَقُلِ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيُؤْمِن وَمَن شَآءَ فَلْيَكْفُرْ
My beloved messnger! You reveal the truth to them about their lord... Then whoever wishes can believe and whoever wishes can disbelieve....
And why take this stance? Because Islam is a religion for the intellect... and the intellect has been given choice... therefore people of the intellect must also be given choice... SubhanAllah!


Then we discussed in the previous session that ..... Aql is also a scale.... It can not only choose between good and evil... it can also choose between two goods and two evils.... Faced with the choice between 2 goods, it can choose the greater good.... and given the choice between 2 evils... it can choose the lesser evil....

Now in life what dictates choice? Knowledge!
We did the exercise in which I compared the intllectual choices between a movie and namaaz as a choice between two goods.... We discussed why choosing to watch a movie was a more intellectually convincing choice over namaaz! I chose watching a movie over namaaz for 2 reasons...
1. Its reward, the pleasure that I will derive from the movie is visible. I have complete knowledge of it through previous expereiences..... and
2. The reward is instant... I can immediately get the reward...

In comparison namaaz lost.... why?
1. because its reward is invisible.... I don't have any tangible experience of that reward .... I have some superficial knowledge of it.... but not a real experience of it.... like I have of watching the movies... because I exactly know what the pleasure of enjoying a movie feels like... but I don't know what the reward of namaaz is going to feel like..
2. The reward is delayed... I have to wait another whatever years before I can die and then get that reward.... that is if such a reward even exists....

So between this comparison obviously... watching the movie wins hands down....

Now for the namaaz to win over the movie.... what are the things that need to change? What would have to change so that given the choice between movie and namaaz... I am willing to choose namaaz over movie....

What is it that will allow me to deny an instant gratification for a delayed one?
The pleasure of namaaz needs to be intellectually visible... it has to be greater than the pleasure of watching the movie....
And the reward.... if it is going to be distant.... it needs to be far far greater than the reward I will gain from watching the movie.
And both.... the pleasure and the reward.... has to be longer lasting than the pleasure of the movie.... Because the only time we are happy to delay our gratification is when? When the reward is far greater than the reward of the instant gratification.... 
But for that there are 2 things involved....
1. a clear knowledge of that reward and
2. the trust that the reward will be delivered to you...

Knowledge and trust! And since WITH knowledge comes trust... therefore knowledge then becomes the key component....
Let's say you have to miss school because of something really important (like new Assassins creed came out) .... but you need the lecture notes for that day... Now can you think of a friend in your class who you can trust to take down those notes for you, if he says he would? And Can you think of a friend who you CANNOT trust to take down notes for you even if he said he would...
Why? What is it that allowed you to make that distinction?

So knowledge about a person... his character... his previous dealings with you or others.... that is what creates trust.. right? Knowledge about the person and his track record! The better the track record, and the more knowledge you have of that track record.... the more you what? Trust that person!

So now if we have to trust Allah what do you need? Knowledge about Allah and knowledge about his track record!

Because does Allah ask us to put our trust in him? OMG ridiculous amounts of trust! Because He wants to make a completely opposite deal with us.. We choose something when?  When the reward is visible and instant, right? But Allah says....



So let's study this ayah piece by piece.... No doubt about it... Allah has purchased from the believers, their souls and their wealth, in exchange for heaven. Who has he purchased the souls of? Not everybody... just believers.... And who are the believers? What are the signs of a believer?  Allah continues to explain



They fight in the cause of Allah? Meaning Jihad? So who are these believers fighting? their own our souls! And in fighting them, what do they do? They kill and are killed.... Kill who? Kill their desires! Kill their greed, their impatience, their anger, their desire for revenge.... their need for instant gratifications... and what else do they do? they get killed? Meaning what? What happens when you are killed? You suffer, right? Is it pleasurable to give up your desires, or is it painful? is it painful to see all your friends partying and having fun and enjoying themselves... and you can't because Allah has forbidden you? Of course it is! Is it painful to hold back your anger even when you have the BEST come back that can make everyone go "OHHH SNAP".... Absolutely! So what is Allah saying?

He's saying ... no doubt Allah has purchased from the believers, their souls and their wealth, in exchange for heaven... And the believers I'm talking about are the ones who fight in the cause of Allah, and they kill or are killed.... Meaning, in following the path of Islam and the teachings of their prophet.... they fight their overwhelming desires, their need for instant gratifications and in the process willingly choose to suffer if they have to....
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
This is a promise that Allah has made compulsory on Himself... The promise of this reward of heaven is something that Allah has made compulsory on Himself.... not just in the Quran, but in the books before it.. the old testamenets and the bible....
وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
And who is more true in his promise than Allah?
This ayah is so beautiful... notice when Allah talked about the promise... he talked about Himself.... not the believers... Meaning he did not use the word PROMSIE when he talked about the believers... he used the word purchase.... but he used the word promise for Himself! Why? Because believers can make mistakes... they can falter.... but Allah does not make mistakes.... He cannot make mistakes... but the believers can.... So even if the believers make mistakes... they falter on their purchase.... Allah says... as long as they keep trying... my purchase, my deal will remain valid.... SubhanAllah.... He imposed perfection on Himself... not the believers...



So this is the first part of the Ayah... Now let's explore this part in a bit more detail.... This ayah begins with a transaction...  a business deal if you will..  إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱشْتَرَىٰ In which Allah has purchased! From whom? مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ from believers... What did he purchase? أَنفُسَهُمْ their souls and وَأَمْوَلَهُم  their wealth, and in return He is giving us what?  What are we receiving for this transaction? بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ We are getting heaven.  This is the exchange Allah talks about in this ayah..

Now what is the ayah teaching us? In order to understand this... let's just talk about the idea of a sale for a moment... Because He is after all talking about a transaction is He not? And a transaction is one in which you sell something and someone purchases what you're selling... Allah says I'm purchasing your soul and your wealth.... The 2 things most dear to all human beings...

Now first of all, can you purchase something from me if I'm not willing to sell it to you? No! So the fact that Allah is purchasing means what? That I'm willing to sell it... You understand? It's a mutual transaction in which both parties are in agreement... It's not a forced transaction...

Now we learnt .... that we human beings... just by nature... we love immediate transactions... what the Quran calls تجارتاً حاضرۃ . right? In other words I pay for something now and I get it when? Right now! The only time we like to delay a transaction, is when we say I want the product now... I want the big screen TV now, but I will pay over a period of 6 months... That's the only time we are fine with delaying our payments.... We like to pay over time..... but receive Immediately... otherwise usually when we pay now, we expect the product right away!

So human beings like to pay late but receive immediately... that's our nature. you agree?

In this ayah, Allah says.. you give me your soul, and you give me your wealth NOW, and in return I will give you heaven...
Now do we like to receive immediately or later? We like to receive immediately. Is Jannah coming immediately or later? It's coming later.... but when do you have to make the payments to Allah? Now! And do you have to make this payment one time or do you have to keep making it everyday? Everyday! We have to keep making those payments with our time, with our wealth, with our prayers, with our fasts, with the way we live, the way we dress, the way we talk, by giving up the things we want to do, but can't do because they're haraam, with the money we can't earn because it's not the permissible way of earning it ... the money we want to spend but it's not the right way to spend that money... and we are constantly holding ourself back because we are in a transaction with Allah...

And we have to constantly remind ourselves that we are doing this in exchange for what? Jannah! But human nature is that if you're paying a price.... if you're making sacrifice to earn something you wanna see it right away! Even if you work 40 hours at a job... atleast 2 weeks later you see a paycheck .... you're going to see something... but when Allah purchases our souls and wealth from us in exchange for Jannah... do we see jannah? NO! All we get is a verbal confirmation from Allah... I'll give it to you... trust me! that's all we get..... nothing else... Allah says.. OK i'll describe it to you in my book... it's got trees, it's got lots of fruits... it's got rivers , you can get married and stuff...  we get a lot of description of jannah.. but do we have a catalog? a broucher, a picture, maybe a video ? A short trailer... just give me a short trailer of jannah ... I don't want the whole movie, just show me like a 30 seconds clip of it.... so that i know what's coming.... nothing! All you get is Allah's word!

Now let's stop here for a second and take a step back and think about what Allah is saying here... He is this salesman, who's coming to us and saying...  Listen, I have this amazing property... prime real estate location... it's like 50 acres, it's a mansion! ... It's worth 50 million, but I'm going to sell it to you for 5000 bucks.... All you have to do is pay me the 5000 now, that too in small tiny installment, and in about 50 or 70 years I'll tell you where that property is... But this deal is only open right now... If you agree... the mansion is yours!

I'll give it to you when? After 50 years... but you have to pay me Right now! Now if any real estate dealer came to us and gave us this deal.... what would be our reaction? We'd be like you sleezy scam artist.... Get outa here... don't waste my time....

But this is Allah... our creator... our master making this deal with us.... Allah is saying I'll give you Jannah, when you're being laid into the ground and there is dirt being thrown on your face.... when you're in the ground... that's when I'll give it to you.... but when do you pay Him? Right now... and we're like ... ummmmmm and he says "Trust Me"... Just trust me.... Doesn't this take an incredible amount of trust?  That's what we mean when we say "believing in the unseen".

ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ۔ وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِٱلْآخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ
That's what we mean when we say we believe and trust Allah... It is not an easy thing.... It is a very easy thing to say it.... that I trust Allah.... but it is a very difficult thing to do... And that's why Allah immediately reassures us...
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
Trust me my slave! Check out my track record from previous books.... I have a great track record... 100% perfection... I have never failed on my promise.... never did... never will.... Trust me!

And why do we need this assurance? Because when you walk this path.... EVERYONE around you will say you are a fool! You're missing out on life! You could have been doing so much more with your youth....  You're wasting your life away with your Islamic values... and that scruffy beard you're trying to grow... You have to go to the masjid and pray and this and that..... we could be having so much fun during that time ...  You only live once! You know...
You're starting your college life... You'll be living in the dorm, your parents won't even be around ... you'll be all by yourself.. There's going to be so much PARTY man! Everyone one is going to have the time of their lives...... I think your lab partner likes you too! It's all good.... Don't worry about it... You're a good lucking guy... you're a beautiful woman... you can have anything and everything you ever wanted.... you have such a beautiful body... you need to show it! You've got  6 pack abbs man.... you need to be more like Salman Khan... take off your shirt for no reason at all.. It's OK... It's not even Ramadan yet..

Now Shaitaan on the other hand gives us the exact opposite deal... He says listen listen! I got some amazing duniya, some luxuries , some incredible pleasures for you... sure they're inferior.... but you can get them RIGHT NOW.... just do this... just drink this... just smoke this....  just steal it, just click on this link.... it's right there.. so easy! Sure You might get into trouble later... but it ain't gonna be right now... you're good! you're fine.! At least for the next 30 50 years there's not going to be any problems ....  And he comes to us and offers us immediately visible reward, with no payments... Obviously far more tempting... far more powerful.. to take immediate reward and leave off what is going to come so far later...

And because we are impulsive buyers... and when a juicy product comes in front of us... we just HAVE to put it in the cart... we can't hold ourselves.... So shaitan makes us the sale and most of us do what? We become customers... we become immediate customers...  But Allah makes us a promise.... and because we don't SEE the product .... so what do we say? eeaaaahhh maybe Ramadan.... that is if the new x-men movie doesn't come out....  I know it's ramadan... but I really wanna go watch the movie... and I can't watch movie without snacks... I'm in such a religious dilemma ... I don't know how to reconcile these two things?

And you say NO... I've made a sale with Allah... I've made a transaction with Allah... And they'll go.... you're SO stupid man! What world are you living in? This is 2015 bro! Why can't you get smart....


And then you keep listening to this.... seeing all this stuff around you and eventually there comes a point where you start feeling ... I can't take this pressure anymore. Everyone is telling me I'm being stupid and missing out on life.... Am I really making a mistake? Am I really missing out on all the fun? Am I really a fool?  Then you open up the Quran and read this ayah and Allah tells you....
فَٱسْتَبْشِرُوا۟ بِبَيْعِكُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَايَعْتُم بِهِ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
Congratulation my believer! You made a great sale! You are the one who is going to experience the ultimate, the greatest of all success....
The only one to congratulate you in these tryin gtimes is not going to be your friends... The only one to congratulate you will be Allah! Your congratulations will come from no where else ... nobody else will think you're making a wise decision.

When you trust Allah... let me tell you... Sometimes the world will stand against you... And you will get so disheartened, so frustrated... you will start losing hope.... At that time you need to grab hold of Allah's book and read it.... and when you read it you will hear Allah saying... Congratulations my slave! You've made an excellent trade...  This is me promising you your reward....

May Allah grant me and you, and everyone of us the wisdom to take His promise seriously... Maybe you've made many many mistakes in the past..... But today you can say... I want to make this sale now... What has happened has happened.... I will not let my past determine my future..... I am willing to choose this path now from today onwards.... And if i fall short... if I make mistakes... it's OK, I will again get back on this deal....

People will give up on you all the time... Best Friends will give up on you.... even your own parents may give up on you... your spouses may give up on you.... but Allah never gives up on you... Allah will never ever give up on you... People will judge you and condemn you.... you know what... you've broken your word too many times... that's it the deal is off!  End of story... but Allah will NEVER NEVER close His doors on you... The only one who can close those doors is you! YOU can close these doors on yourself.... Allah will never do it! SubhanAllah!

And this is what I want to show you kids in these session.... what Allah wants for us.... How much he loves us... How much he cares for us.... How He is the ONLY true friend you and I will have.... But for that we need to gain this knowledge from the Quran... so that the reward of afterlife becomes visible to us... and we understand how the reward is so much greater than the fleeting instant pleasures of this world....

Because this world is DUNIYA.... which I told you comes from the word "dana" meaning low (as in easily accessible, instant) but inferior... Whereas AKHIRAH comes from the word Khair which means superior... So Hayaat AlDuniya means The Inferior life even though it is instant..... whereas Haayat AlAkhirah means The Superior life, even though it comes later.... But everything worth anything always demands patience....

Yes... the rewards of this deal come later... but if you honestly notice... the rewards of everything worth anything are always received later....

School life compared to professional life is inferior or not? Why? Because you're dependant.... and professional life is superior why? Because you're independant! But to achieve this independant superior life... where must you strive hard? Where must you make sacrifices of pleasures? In your school life....
Being an adult is better or being a child? An adult! But you have to invest in those 18 years of training to reap its rewards laster....
Is it better to have one mango.... or is better or having a tree of mangoes ? Having a tree of course! loaded with mangoes.... but do you get that instantly as soon as you plant the seeds or do you have to wait for it? You have to wait for it? But is the reward manyfold greater than that instant gratification of one mango? Absolutely!
Is it better to cut open the goose to get all the golden eggs today? Or feed that goose and keep it alive and delay that gratification by getting one golden egg everyday?
Is it better to enjoy all the money you have today... or is it wiser to invest it in stocks... or long term saving accounts in order to collect double or tripple the amount of the original investment?
So the question before me and before you is..... Are these few temporary years of this duniya... of this inferior life better? Or is the everlasting superior life of Akhira better? Akhira! But where do you have to strive in order to reap the rewards in that superio life? In this inferior life

وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَىْءٍۢ فَمَتَعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا ۚ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
And whatever thing you have been given - it is only for the enjoyment of this inferior life and its adornment. And what is with Allah is so much better and ever lasting;
And then Allah asks us...
  أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ so why don't you understand?  What tool do you need to understand or reason? The intellect! SubhanAllah!

بَلْ تُؤْثِرُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا؟ وَٱلْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌۭ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ
Do You really prefer the life of this inferior world? Even though the after life is so much better and ever lasting!
إِنَّ هَذَا لَفِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ۔ صُحُفِ إِبْرَهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ
I've said these same things in the previous books....in the books of Ibrahim and Moosa... This is not something new I'm telling to you about..... I've always been telling you this.... from the beginning of time that the afterlife is so much better than the life of this world.... why don't you guys understand? When will you understand?






 
We're understanding the message of Allah from the Quran... and the tool that Allah has blessed us with.... in order for us to understand his message is the intellect... So we've started with the study of the intellect or عقل... So what have we learnt so far about عقل?

1. It is a container for knowledge that expands as the knowledge increases
2. It was the first thing that Allah created... This is the first sentence of the hadees I shared with you.... This is the statement with which the hadees begins.... اول ما خلق اللہُ العقل Now why is that important? If you have a lot of tasks that you need to complete for school.... what do you do? You prioritize them, right? What are the things you take into consideration when setting your priorities? Urgency and Importance could be one of them... length of the tasks in comparison to the available time... But the first thing you do, is what? create the task list itself before you begin working on the tasks....
Let's say you have to write 6 different papers for your school.... You have all the reference books stacked up on your table... you have a list of all the links to the sites that you're going to need... You have your order set in terms of which paper you will write first... You have your supply of cookies and your cadburry eggs next to you.... you've chosen the tunes you're going to play as you work on these papers... But the main instrument that you need to begin writing is what? A pen! If you don't have a pen... does anything else matter?? .... except the cadburry eggs of course? No! It's the very first thing you need if you want to write you papers....

Similarly Allah created the heaves and the earths and everything that exists in it... He created these mansions upon mansions in heaven for his creations.... He planned out his messengers... I'm going to send Adam first, then Nooh, then Ibrahim, then Moosa, then Eisa (علیھم السلام). And then finally I'm going to send my beloved prophet Muhammad (SAS) with this extremely elaborate.... extremely eloquent and symmetrically miraculous message for my creations so that they can understand it and earn my reward and get closer to me and my love... oh wait... what is the first thing they need in order to do that? The intellect! Without the intellect nothing else is going to matter! without the intellect he's not going to recognize my messenger, nor his message... He will not be able to see the miracles in my Quran that I've placed for him...and without the message he will not be able to come close to me.... he will not be able to get into this beautiful paradise that I've created for him...

Therefore what was the first thing that Allah created? The intellect! SubhanAllah!

Now if intellect is the first thing Allah created.... what is the first thing we should be using to understand his religion? The intellect!
