So now you tell me... how can we settle for responses like.... don't use your intellect... don't think too much about religion... it'll drive you nuts.... things like that? How could we accept that line of thinking?
The reason Allah created the intellect first is so that before any decision we make in our lives... we present it before our intellect first? Is this going to benefit me in the long run? Or is it going to hurt me in the long run? Should I grab this instant pleasure right before me? Or should I go for the eternal pleasure that is to come....
So intellect is the first thing Allah created....
We also learnt that it is the most lofty thing that Allah created for the purpose of obedience. It had the potential to obey or disobey Allah... It had the potential to compromise and it had the potential to stand its ground... And Allah gave it complete freedom of choice...
Intellect is a scale..... it can not only choose between good and bad, it can also choose between two goods and two evils. In the last session I talked in detail about this....

The way in which the intellect works is that when faced with 2 good choices.... it chooses the pleasure or the benefit that is visible and instantly accessible... The only time it chooses something not visible and something accessible later is when...
1. When there is knowledge of the reward that is to come in the future... and absolute trust in the one who has promised the reward
2. When the benefit and the reward that is to come... far exceeds the benefit of the instant gratification

And based on this trust we accepted the deal that Allah made with us....
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱشْتَرَىٰ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوَلَهُم بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ
We sold our souls and our wealth to Allah...... as believers... in exchange for his promise of heaven....
يُقَتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ
We agreed to the terms and conditions of being a believer as Allah imposed on us.... my believers are those who will sacrifice their worldly desires, their visible and instantly gratifying desires for me.... and they will be willing to suffer for it.... It's not going to be easy for them.... There will be times when killing their desires will mean literally cutting their heart out.... but they will do it... for me
وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّۭا فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ وَٱلْقُرْآنِ ۚ وَمَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ
And they can trust me... because I have made this promise of reward mandatory for myself in not only the Quran but all the previous books... to all previous nations.... I'm not asking my believers to promise me that they will never break this deal.... In fact, I am promising them that I will give them this reward no matter how many times they break this deal with me..... just as long as they sincerely and honestly keep trying.... As long as they constantly return to this deal with me.... As long as they don't take undue advantage of my love and mercy.... I am promising you... I will give you this reward...
And when this world forces you to break this deal with me; when your friends force you to break this deal with me; when your family forces you to break this deal with me.... when your desires force you to break this deal with me.... when everyone one around you is calling you a loser... a fool... a fanatic... a coward... and you can't take it anymore.... you can't bear the pressure, you can't bear the pain of those insults.... those criticisms and sarcasms any more.... in that moment of weakness remember my words
فَٱسْتَبْشِرُوا۟ بِبَيْعِكُمُ ٱلَّذِى بَايَعْتُم بِهِۦ وَذَلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ
In that moment.... rejoice in the faith... feel congratulated by Me.... your Master and your lord... who loves you more than anyone in this world... who loves you more than your mother, your father, your family, your kids, your spouse, your friends.... all of them COMBINED.... feel congratulated by Him on this amazing deal that you've made.... because you will be the successful one! You will achieve the great fortune....  ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ.

Did we discuss these words  ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ in the last session?

The word فوز in Arabic comes from the word فاز. which means success, or a gain, or some good fortune.... But in ancient or classical Arabic, the word فوز is used, when you succeed, or are victorious.... or you gain something.... meeting two specific conditions ..... If either one of these conditions is not met... then it is not called فوز ...
The first condition is that whatever you've achieved... you then never want to part with.... EVER! Not even for a second.... that's the first condition... but that's not all... what you've achieved... as part of فوز..... it should also make you the object of desire for others.... it has to make you the object of envy for others..... When others look at you, they should desperately WANT what you have.... they should go... OMG... I'd give absolutely ANYTHING in a heartbeat to get what s/he has.... that's when the word فوز  is used.....
So there are two nuances to the word فوز .... You have to love it so much that you never want to part with it... and others should crave what you have... only when both these conditions are met... that's when you have فوز

And in this deal... Allah has promised the believer فوز .... He said... if you come through on this deal with Me... I promise... that what I will give you... it will not only make you so happy that you will NEVER EVER want to part from it... it'll also be SO AWESOME that everyone around you today... your friends.. your family... your loved ones.. your enemies... EVERYONE will wish they had what you've been given...

Another beautiful derivation of the word فوز ... is فازَۃٌ, which to the Arabs meant .... an extremely comfortable tent in the middle of a scorching desert.... Now imagine you're an Arab wandering in the scorching heat of the desert..... very thirsty, hungry and tired... ... And you find this nice and cool tent... containinng all the comforts you need.... water, food, comfortable bed... big screen tv... What would that tent, that comfort mean to you at that time? Once you enter it, would it not also satify the previous 2 conditions of the word فوز? Because it would be something you would never want to part with... you'd never want to go back in the scorching heat of the desert.... and all others still in the scorching heat will be craving for what you have gained!

But then in this ayah.... Allah did not just promise us فوز  did he? He promised ... فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ. .... Allah tacked the word عظیم with it.... If I start going into the linguistic nuances of this words... we'll never see the end of this discussion on intellect.... So I'll tell you one small meaning quickly... The word عظیم comes from the word عَظُم, which is also the root word for عظام  which means "bone".... Now what are the attributes of a bone?? It is solid right? Its solid, it's stable, it's strudy... it's reliable... the entire human structure stands upon it... but then there is the word عظامۃ, as a verb, which literally means: when the bone grows strong.... like the bones of a growing child.... you know how a child's bones become stronger as he grows.. So combine it with فوز, the suggestion is a success whose pleasure increases.... and becomes stronger with the passage of time....
Now from a grammatical stand point, the words فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ  in this ayah are used as a noun infinitve phrase.... In grammar an infinitive phrase is something that extends infinitely and indefinitely... something that is subject to no limitation... For example the phrase...

1. Everyone wants to enjoy life.... is a noun infinitive phrase... It's continuous and it is infinitive... The sentence suggest that there will not come a time when everyone will not want to enjoy life... So is the phrase....
2. She likes to be admired. - the desire for admiration is an infinite thing indefinite thing...

So the grammatical use of  فَوْزُ عَظِيمُ  in this ayah suggests that this reward that you achieved, it is not only so amazing that you don't want to ever part with it.. it's not only something that makes you the envy of others... it is not only growing strong as time passes.... but also that its growth is infinite... There's not going to come a time when this reward stops increasing... EVER!!! So how much will the pleasure of this reward increase? It will keep growing.... and growing and growing... !!! Infinitely!!! SubhanAllah.... 

Now you tell me... Isn't this فوز عظیم a great reward?

We said ... the only time we are willing to sacrifice instant gratifications is when? When the reward we are going to get later is much much much greater than the pleasure we are sacrificing right now, correct? You tell me honestly... no matter what we sacrifice in this lifetime... isn't this reward from Allah..... this فوز عظیم that you love so much... and you can't part with... that makes you the  envy of everyone around you because of how magnificent it is... one that grows infinitely ... beyond measure....  and its pleasure never stops increasing... isn't this a greater reward than any sacrifice we will ever make in this world? It certainly is....

But then Allah did not just sayٌفوزٌ عظیم did he? He said الفوز العظیم. He added the ال to both words.... To both فوز and عظیم. AllahuAkbar... ال in Arabic is called علامت المعرفۃ. When you want to make something specific.. like not just any book but a particular book... Not just any car but a particular car.... that's one reason when you add ال  to a word in Arabic... to make it specific....
Allah says for this deal that you're making.... I'm not going to give you فوز, I will give you الفوز.... That ever increasing reward that I just descibed.. it will be specific to you.... Nobody else in my entire creation... will have the experience YOU are going to have... Each one of you will have your own individual experience....
And that experience is not just going to be عظیم, it's going to be العظیم. It's not going to keep increasing infinitely in any generic way... It's going to increase in a way specifically tailored for you.... It'll infinitely increase in the specific way you want it to increase in.... 

This is what I love about the Arabic language.... can you see the depth of this language in just 2 words!

We should all... each and everyone of us.... memorize this ayah with full comprehension... because there are so many times when we need to remind ourselves of this....


What else does the word عقل mean?
It is a restraint! Another meaning for the word عقل in ancient arabic, is to tie or restrain a camel with a strong rope.... And the rope used to tie the camel was called عقال  . Now let's explore the meaning of عقل as a restraint....
Now a camel to an Arab, is what a car is to a teenager.... Your car, no matter what it looks like... what year, make or model it is.... it's precious to you, correct?... That's why you spit shine it twice a day.... right? Now if you love your car so much, would you be worried about its safety?  Of course.... you would be! Why? Because it's YOUR car! It's your baby.... You love it... and it is also your responsibility.... ONLY you can take care of it in the best possible way! You'll make sure it stays in good condition... you get the oil changes done regularly... you take care of its service and maintenance needs.... And all this stems from your sense of ownership and also your attachment to it....

This is what a camel was to the Arab.... It was not only his ride, it was not only its source of livelihood.... it was also his closest friend in most cases.... because these were bedouins who scaled the deserts... They didn't spend as much time with their wives and children as much as they did with their camels... Believe it or not.... they actually wrote long poems about their camels... about its eyes, the way it moved... which should now be of no surprise to you.... because after all poetry was their thing.... Poetry to them.... was what conversation is to us......

So now imagine you've time travelled 2000 years back in time, and your parents just bought you your very own camel... This camel is now your new BFF! So you ride into town on your new camel to buy some groceries.... When you reach the market.... you park your camel outside.... you dismount it...  Now what do you have to do before you enter the market? No you don't pull the hand break!!
You have to tie the camel! Why? Why do you need to tie the camel? Why can't you just leave it there?  Because your camel doesn't know what's good for it and what's not... If you don't tie it... it may wander off..... someone else will find it..... and a few hours later... your best friend might end up on a huge platter.... all nice and tender... possible? But not even just that... you can't actually take care of a camel in anyway until you tie it... You can't feed it...... You can't bathe and clean it...... You can't treat it when it's sick or hurt...... You can't shelter it from the storm... You can't do any of this until you have tied it up first .... Why? Because first you HAVE to restrain it... get it under your control... and then do whatever you need to do to take care of it... You understand? Same thing with your car!

Therefore I argue that restraint is compulsory... its required for safety.... and the benefit of the one being restrained.... Intellect is a restraint... Until you set a boundary... you cannot tackle anything in life... That's why we have to-do lists.... that's why we have specific subjects in school... taught in a particular time frame... We restrain subjects within time frames.....  That's why we have 9-5 work boundaries... That's why we build houses in which we confine ourselves for our safety and our benefit... Everything we do in life... we can do in life is only possible because of some form or restraint or boudary....
Even the universe works on the principle of restraint... Thats why we have seasons bound in a particular time durations... That's why we have a duration for day and a duration for night...  Imagine if day and night happened randomly... our entire life would be destroyed... we would not be able to take advantage of the day, nor would we be able to take advantage of the night...
There is nothing in this world, that can function without a restraint.... In order to give benefit or take benefit... some sort of restraint is required... And anything that goes out of restraint, goes out of control...... and anything that goes out of control is disasterous... What happens when someone loses restraint or control over your car? Accidents! What happens when you lose control over your balance? You fall! What happens when the gas coming to your stove is not restrained or controlled? What happens when the flame of the fire is not restrained, not controlled when you cook your food???
And what happens when nature releases its restraint over its elements like wind and water? You get hurricanes and sunamis...
Therefore I argue that restraint is an essential component to physical human existence...

Does everyone agree with me on this??? Any questions about this?


So returning to the camel... the reason you tie your camel is for its safety.... You want to keep it controlled so that you can keep it safe because you care about it, because its YOUR camel... it's your responsibility... So there are two reasons for tying your camel: love and justice.... You keep it restrained because you love it and you want to care of it... and you keep it restrained because you don't want the camel to hurt others... Not let it harm other peoples crops and property! And this applies to everything in life... Love for self... justice for others... You agree?

However... by tying the camel, did you limit it's freedom? Did you restrict its movement? You did!.... But was it absolutely necessary for you to do that? Yes of course!  Now do you think the camel always likes that? As soon as you reached the market.... does the camel jump up and down and say... Yay! tie me up! Tie me up!! :)) Probably not... But nevertheless you tie it up because you understand that the restraint is necessary for its safety and the safety of others... whether the camel understands it or not... whether it appreciates it or not.... whether it likes it or not... You are doing what you're doing to keep it and others safe..

But yes! Tying the camel does limits the camels freedom... See the thing with safety is..... it is directly proportional to the level of restraint.... or it is inversely proportional to freedom....
Meaning the higher the level of safety required.... the stronger the restraint will be.... To tie the camel you're not going to just use a piece of string, will you? No! You'll use a strong enough rope, right? The restraint will be in proportion to strength of the camel...
And as the level of safety increases... the level of freedom will be limited... The stronger you tie the camel... the more restricted and limited its movement will be...

You sit in your car... what is the first thing you do? You put your seat belt on.... Now as soon as you put it on.... does it restrict your movement to a certain extent? Yes it does.... It doesn't completely incapacitate you, but it does limit your movement! You can still move... just not as freely... But does that restraint save you in the case of an accident? It does InshaAllah!
What about when you sit on a roller coaster ride? Does it completely restrict your movement? It does! Why? because a much much higher level of safety is required!


What about traffic lights? Do they limit freedom? Of course! You have to wait till the light turns green! But are these lights essential to the safety of drivers? Yes!
What happens at airports? You have to go through all these security checks .... does it limit your freedom a LOT? Absolutley.... But those controls are restrictions are required for your and my safety .... as well as the safety of other passengers.... And the greater the level of safety required, the higher the level of what? restrictions!


So now is Islam a restrictive religion? You bet it is! It is definitely a restrictive religion... but why? Because of safety! Whose safety? Your and my safety... Your safety individually and our safety  as a group collectively... We are Allah's creation... We are his camels... Rasul Allah is our shepherd and Islam is the rope of Allah that Rasul Allah (SAS) has used to tie us... so that he can keep us safe.... individually as well as collectively...
Have you ever seen how a rope is made? It is made from two strings twined firmly together.... Allah's rope is made of Islam and Iman twined together.... And he says in the Quran  وَٱعْتَصِمُوا۟ بِحَبْلِ ٱللَّهِ جَمِيعًۭا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا۟ Hold fast to the rope of Allah and do not get divided.... what is that rope of Allah? It is the rope of of Islam and Iman...

Islam is a restrictive religion because it is a religion of safety... And there cannot be safety unless there is restraint....  The Arabic word Islam comes from the root word سلم. which has many different meanings... InshaAllah we'll explore all of them at a later time.... One of those meanings is to surrender willingly  and completely... willingly and completely!
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُۥ رَبُّهُۥٓ أَسْلِمْ .  قَالَ أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ ٱلْعَلَمِينَ
His master said to Ibrahim AS, surrender! He said I completely and willingly surrender to the master of the worlds
And the one who willingly and completely surrenders himself.... his will.... his desires.... his ego to Allah is called a Muslim
أَلَّا تَعْلُوا۟ عَلَىَّ وَأْتُونِى مُسْلِمِينَ
Don't be arrogant before me... Come to me in submission as a Muslim
Another meaning of the word سلام is safety...
قُلْنَا يَا نَارُ كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
We ordered the fire to become cold and safe for Ibrahim....

So 2 meanings out of many other meanings for the word سلام  are what? Submission and Safety! And how do you become safe? By completely surrending yourself to your master... Can you tie your camel if it keeps struggling? No! The camel needs to surrender to your rope... to your عقال  in order for you to keep it safe.... We are Allah's camels... And Rasul Allah (SAS), is our shepherd..... Islam is his rope that he ties us with.... We need to surrender to the rope he has given us... We need to accept him as our shepherd and surrender to the rope of Islam if we desire OUR safety and if we desire OUR success... Why? Because there can never be safety without restraint.... and we need to surrender willingly and completely to that restraint if we want to be safe.. Safety from what?  From satanic temptations.... Because what happens when a camel starts wandering? It will wander into every single valley that draws its attention....ٰIt'll get lost... It'll get killed...

The rope of Islam forms a secure boundary all around us for our safety, so that Allah.... who is our master can take care of us... He can give us the benefits and the rewards and the success he desires for us....  If we do not surrender ourselves to this boundary of Islam... If we do not secure ourselves within this boundary of Islam.... completely and willingly we are going to lose our way to Allah.... We will wander away from him....
The reason we feel Islam's restrictions so much on us today is because of the times we live in.... Look at the temptations that surround us.... the external pressures we all are exposed to! Outside the restraint of Islam you have magazines and media and people that tell you that you have to dress a certain way... look a certain way... talk a certain way... behave a certain way... If you don't do that you're not cool... you're old fashioned... you're stupid.... We constantly keep feeling this pull while we are tied to the rope of Islam... Of course it's difficult....

But the question before the intellect is... what is the right choice? Instant gratification? Or الفوز العظیم?? Because we are surrounded by satanic temptations all around us.... Everything that is COOL today..... we find it's against the teachings of Islam!! In every dimension of our lives.... The only thing protecting us is the rope of Allah.... the rope of Islam... The only way to save ourselves is to surrender willingly and completely to this عقال ... this restraint of Allah which is Islam....

If a hurricane is coming... what do you do? You surrender yourself to a house... a shelter... that you know has a solid foundation... You admit that I am not strong enough to face this storm by myself.... I need to take shelter in a structure that is stronger than me.....  so that IT can keep me safe... You submit yourself and your freedom in exchange for the protection and safety of that shelter... Yes? What'll happen if you try to stand and face the storm and say... Aahhh I can take it on! I ain't scared of it! How long will you last? But once you're in the shelter are you restricted? Do you have to give up some of your freedom for your safety? Of course!

And what'll happen if once inside the shelter..... you want to keep the doors and windows open?? Yeah I know there is a storm outside... but it's so suffocating in here... let me just open the window.. let me just keep the door open for a nice breeze... It's OK... it's not like I'm standing outside in the open... I'm just one step outside the door! I just have my neck ouside the door.... it's not the end of the world... Don't be so paranoid!

I know Islam forbids it, but so what if I eat a little bit of crab, a little bit of lobster, mushrooms... so what? So what if I smoke... at least its not alcohol.... what's the big deal.... I know what Islam says about clothing, but so what if I dress provocatively... what's the big deal..... it 2015! What's the problem?
Well no problem to Islam really!..... The shelter will still be there AFTER the storm has passed.... The shelter is not the one going to get hurt.... It's you that will get hurt!  Becuase you violated the rules of safety!


Islam is a religion of restraint... a restraint put on us by our master for our souls and intellect..... It is a restraint that Allah placed on us so that we remain connected to Him... because he loves us.... because he cares about us.... He does not want us to wander away from him... He does not want us to leave Him....  Just as we restrict a camel because we care about it and we don't want it to wander away and get lost....  Allah says...

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
Do you not see that they wander aimlessly in every valley? كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
يَهِيمُونَ comes from the word ھاما. ھاما is used for a camel when it gets lost in the desert looking for water... It goes in one valley, then another valley, then another valley  searching for water.. aimlessly waandering looking for water... and finally dies of thirst... When the Arabs used the word ھاما it meant that it is pointless waiting for the camel to return, because it is never going to return... This is how Allah describes those of us wandering trying to find a connection elsewhere....

We should really examine our own lives and the lives of those around us in the context of this ayah... We accomplish so much in this world, but why is it that the desires of our heart never end? Some spend their entire time on facebook, whatsapp, all these social sites... desperately trying to find a connection... but the heart keeps longing for more .... constantly keeps searching for more! And no matter how hard we try... why just can't seem to find that perfect connection... We continuously have that feeling of emptiness... that void in our hearts no matter how hard we try to fill it! Some try to fill that emptiness with money, some with power, some try to drown it in alcolhol, some try to forget it with drugs.... but that emptiness is always there... If money is the key to all happiness then why are so many rich people committing suicides? I can understand if the poor runs after wealth, but why do we see the filthy rich people constantly running after money as well? The guy is a millionaire, but his heart still wants more? He wants to be billionarie... Why is that? What is he searching for? Even with all the money, all the comforts of this world... why is his heart never satisfied? Why is it always looking for more? Because they're wandering aimlessly in the valley of greed and deception....
فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ

There was a sage who had the knowledge of turning straw into gold... A man begged him to teach him that skill.... The sage asked him... what will you do with all the gold you make? He said I'll become very rich.... The sage asked why do you want to be rich? He said so I can live a very comfortable and peaceful life.... So the sage said, then it is not gold that you seek... what you really seek is comfort and peace for you heart....  Wouldn't it be better if I taught you how to find peace and comfort for your heart instead of teaching you how to make gold?

Today we have people who think that we gold is the key to finding peace and comfort... it is not.... You can make as much gold as you want.... your heart will never find comfort from it... Yes you can distract it for a while with luxurous items, jewllery, branded cars, big mansions.... but this hunger...this thirst of the heart will never go away.... because the heart is not searching for gold... it's searching for something else....

Look at the artists in the music industry. How they wander and lose themselves in the valley of desire and fame.... The artists starts off with a desire for their song to be heard... She gets a break, someone produces his album... everybody downloads it... it goes platinum... they become extremely popular, extremely wealthy in one night as a result... She wins a music award! Now is that the end of his career? No! Now she has to come up with the next big hit.... but the next album she releases is not as entertaining.... not as successful... not as many sales... didn't make it to the top of the charts... Now she starts feeling desperate.... she starts seeing her own decline.... what does every artist want? Even  more than money? Fame! Appreciation, Acknowledgement, recognition! They live off of that... but instead of that now she's getting criticisms... rotten tomatos, one star, thumbs down.... she starts getting depressed... so she starts thinking what can i do to get back on top? I need to come up with a new song... wait! I already tried that... didn't work out too well... Maybe if my new song was a little controvertial... maybe if it was a little more indecent.. it might get more popular... does that happen? Yes it does!
And then when that doesn't work as well then maybe if I drop my standards of decency even further... maybe that'll get me there... If that doesn't happen... maybe I'll just shave my head... get drunk.. get arrested... adopt a baby... adopt 12 babies....Maybe if I can't be mentioned at the award ceremony.. maybe I'll be mentioned in the tabloid at the end of a grocery aisle. At least I'll be mentioned somewhere... Does that happen? Sure! They get more and more and more desperate... willing to try anything... when nothing works, they switch to alcolhol, then drugs and then one day they're found dead in the bathtub of their penthouse suite....

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ

And most importantly, what does this "need for" more indicate?  It indicates that the heart is not getting what it truly needs....
Let's say you have some critical data on your phone that you need for school... You don't have a backup of it... the only original copy you have of it is on your phone.... That data is so important, that if you don't have it you can't function at school....  Now what happends when you lose your phone with all that data on it? What do you start doing? You start searching for it! Where do you look? Everywhere.... in your house... in your friends house... the places you visited... places which absolutely make no sense to search in.... but you do...  you keep searching endlessly for it.... Now I come to you and give you a brand new model of your phone... a much more beautiful, more powerful phone.... will that satisfy you? No! It does not have the content you need...
Now can I distract you temporarily... take your mind off the search for a bit.... right? I can distract you with facebook, whatsapp, hanging out with friends, PS4, Xbox etc....  but the moment the distraction is over... what do you do? You start searching for you phone again right? Now there is only one way in which this need... this search can end... What is that? It is If you find your phone.... Once you find it, will you search for it anymore? Of course not!

You see... the problem is that all these worldly luxuries can provide comfort to our bodies.... but not to our hearts... Because the heart was never meant for this world... And we constatnly try to satisfy its needs with worldly posessions... money, fame, luxuries, all sorts of jewellery and bling... But its need is not physical in nature.... The body's need is physical.... The hearts need is spiritual.... and until it finds the object of its search, the heart will always remain dissatisifed... And the heart is hungry because we have broken the rope of Allah... the عقال of Allah that he used to tie us... to keep us close to him... We broke that rope and moved away from him... And now this heart longs for Allah... it is searching for Allah... it looks for Allah every where.... wanders in every valley trying to find him... Thinks that it can replace the need for the love of Allah with gold and money, and fame and power and distractions.... but nothing satisfies it... Why doesn't it find satisfaction.... because these material things do not bring peace to the heart... Then what does? Allah answers this questions in such simple words in the Quran...
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Beware! Only by the rememberance of Allah, your the heart finds peace...
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Allah is the ultimate goal of our hearts... Until it finds Allah, the heart does not find peace... Cannot find peace... Like a fish out of water, it yearns to return back to it... our hearts crave for the love of Allah.... Like a man thirsty for water is not satisfied with anything else.... he is not distracted by anything except water...  you can give him mountains of gold and silver... all the power, fame... nothing can satisfy his thirst until he finds water....
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ


 

Ramadan Mubarak...  Happy fathers day

Allah created the intellect العقل as a container for knowledge, and by design kept it limitless..... عقل I learnt means an elastic container that expands to fit the contents place in it.....
Then we learnt from Rasul Allah (SAS)'s hadees that Allah created العقل  as His very first creation, before anything else..... He gave it precedence over all other creation, ..... so that we would give it precedence too and use our intellects before we make any decisions in life...
He gave us our intellect complete choice of freedom so that it may choose freely and willingly and move towards Allah's obedience.... People confuse freedom of choice with the consequences of choices.... It is important that we choose well because every choice has a consequence which comes packaged with it.... You pick up one end of the sick... you automatically pickup the other.. Islam gives people the choice to choose the path that they want to follow and nobody is compelled to follow any particular path... However each path has a destination... it ends in a different place...
لَا يَسْتَوِىٓ أَصْحَبُ ٱلنَّارِ وَأَصْحَبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ أَصْحَبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ هُمُ ٱلْفَآئِزُونَ
The people of heaven and the people of hell cannot be the same... it is the people of heaven that have great success...

We learnt that العقل  also means a scale... a scale to not just choose between good and bad..... but especially between two goods and two evils... so that we choose the greater of two goods and the lesser of two evils...
Finally العقل is  also meant as a restraint....  He gave us the intellect so that we could use it as a tool to restrain our desires and passions.... so that we could establish a boundary of safety... so that we could protect ourselves and the people around us.... So that we could live a controlled life of obedience... instead of an uncontrolled life of disobedience....
I also proved to you through the example of a camel and his rope.... that happiness and comfort for not just human beings.... but all creation can only exist within confines.... within certain boundaries and parameters.... And when desires and passions of the heart cross these boundaries.... and step outside of these boundaries.... the soul gets lost... and then like a lost camel searching for water..... wanders aimlessly into every valley trying to find the object of its desires..... 
أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍۢ يَهِيمُونَ
but the desires never end.... after every desire stands another desire, and then another.... After every valley there is another valley.... the soul keeps chasing them and finally loses itself.... even loses the path to return to its master....

Intellect means restraint... Intellect means discipline.. it means to train ourselves to follow or obey certain rules or code of behavior....

Islam is a religion of restrictions..... Islam is a religion of disciplines... why? Because it is a religion of safety and it is a religion of comfort... And what did we study about the relationship between restrain and safety? The more safety is required, the greater the restraint must be.... The greater the restraint... the lesser the freedom.. Safety and comfort is directly proportional to restraint..... and inversely proportional to freedom.... Islam is a religion of safety... it is therefore a religion of restraints... It is therefore a religion of disciplines... It was desgined by the Creator.... for His creation.... so that they would find peace and comfort by it....

When we enter the walls or confines of Islam, we find several restraints or disciplines within it.... we find 7 primary disciplines.... There is Wilayah, taharat, salaah, zakaat, saum, hajj, jehad.... These are the restraints or disciplines within islam... So if you think of Islam as a house, then think of these disciplines as pillars... The structure of Islam stands on these pillars or these disciplines.... If there are no pillars ... there can be no structure.... when the pillars are weak ... the structure is weak... right?

So the structure of Islam is based on 7 sub structures or disciplines.... Taharat and Salaah are the disciplines of a day - because at the least they are done 5 times a day; zakaat and fasting are the disciplines of a year... because they are done once a year....  Hajj is a discipline that is done once in a lifetime... Wilayah which means love, and Jihad which means struggle are the disciplines of every single moment... they are the disciplines of every single breadth... because every moment of our life should be filled with the love of our master... and every moment of our life should be filled with our struggle... our strive... our efforts to please Him...

And only through these restraints... through these disciplines....  the soul and the heart can find peace, comfort and safety.... 

So today I want to talk about the discipline of Ramadan..... I was going to talk about this at a later time... but since we're going through this holy month, I figured this would be the best time to talk briefly about it...

When we think of fasting... our first thought is about hunger and thirst....  The things that come to mind when we talk about salaah (namaaz) is wuzu, ghusl... qayam, ruku, sujood... when we think about zakaat what comes to mind is the loss of money.... So there are certain actions or attributes associated with each discipline... But here's a very interesting thing about Islam and all it's disciplines.... Nothing is what it seems :)
When Allah talks about the Quran He says...
 إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌۭ وَقُرْآنٌۭ مُّبِينٌۭ


We recite to you the signs and the Wise Remembrance.

Allah says the Quran is a reminder.... everything about Islam and all it's disciplines are actually a reminder for something else....

So fasting has actually nothing to do directly with hunger and thirst... the hunger and thirst are a reminder for something else.....
the qayam, ruku and sujood even though a part of namaaz... are actually a reminder for something else..... these are all symbols.... and a symbol points to that which is symbolized..... You see a half bitten apple logo on your phone.... it reminds you that it was made by the Apple Company... you see flying windows... it reminds you of Microsoft... because symbols are reminders for that which they symbolize....


So today I want to talk to you about what fasting is....  and what the hunger and thirst symbolize.... Hunger and thirst are not the purpose of fasting.... That would just not make sense... Why would Allah prevent us from eating and drinking??? Why would he have a problem with us for eating and drinking??? He really doesn't.... But he created fasting for us.... and stopped us from eating and drinking during the day... as a symbol for us to be reminded of something greater!!! And if our fast constantly reminds us of that which it points to... then we are actually fasting..... otherwise all we are just simply dieting... you understand?

Questions?

So I want to talk to you about a couple of things today about the discipline of fasting ..... especially in the context of restraint...

A nation is known by its captial... when you have a nation you have to have a captial, especially if you're a big nation. The capital becomes an integral part of your identity... If one city in a country falls to the enemy... or the enemy takes over one city or one village, or one town... no big deal... when do you know that the country is completely defeated? when the enemy takes over the capital.... When the enemy takes over the captial... the country is no more... the capital is considered the identity of the nation.... the qibla for the Muslims is our identity... it is our captial.... The Ka'aba is our Islamic capital... We all face in its direction...

The qibla before Islam used to be Bait AlMaqdis for the Jews.... When Allah changed that qibla.. .he changed our identiy.... he changed our identity... As soon as he changed our qibla he said
وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَكُمْ أُمَّةًۭ وَسَطًۭا
That is how we made you a middle nation
He innaugurated us as a new nation by changing our captial... giving us our own captial.

Now an area near the outskirts of a country is never selected to be the capital of the country... why? Because like I just said... once the capital is conquered.... the country is no more... so the capital is usually in the inner most city of a country.... it is well protected from all sides.. The goverment operates from within the capital.... the decision making bodies operate from within the captial....
If we think of our body as a country.... what is our capital?? The heart! The heart is the capital of our body.... It is located in the inner most part of our body... well surrounded from all sides.... it governs all our decisions.... It decides what we will do and what we will not do... you understand? I want you to keep this concept in mind for our conversation today...

 

So now lets start talking about fasting...  This fasting is not something that was given specifically to the Muslims.... The Quran says...
يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلصِّيَامُ كَمَا كُتِبَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
Oh Believers! we have enforced fasting on you (made it mandatory on you) just as we had enforced it on those that came before you so that you can become pious....

So Allah says that we had made fasting mandatory on those who came before you .... who is allah talking about? The jews! The people of bani-israeel... the Isrealites...The shareeat of Mussa (AS). He says we made fasting compulsory for you just as we made it compulsory for the people of Israeel..... and why did we give them fasting then, and why are we giving you fasting now....
لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
So that YOU people can become pious... so that you can have taqwa..

Now this is very interesting... If you read the first half of Surah AlBaqarah, you'll find that Allah talks about the jews... and Allah complains the entire time about them... that why don't you have taqwa? the entire time.... the problem is that they have no taqwa... And now Allah says you ... the Muslims.... are given the same exercise as them... which is what? Fasting! and what's the point of all of it... so that YOU can have taqwa.... Allah in this ayah explains the purpose of fasting .... why is it that we fast in the first place....لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ  to acquire taqwa...

I want to talk to you about it from a practical stand point what that means....

One of the meanings of Taqwa is to fear Allah... to be cautious of Allah....  Taqwa comes from the word وقایا which actually means protection.... Taqwa is similar to اتقا to seek protection .... to try to protect yourself... That's why on judgment day... everyone will try to protect themselves... nobody will care about anybody else... they will all try to protect only themselves..... so allah says
فَكَيْفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرْتُمْ يَوْمًۭا يَجْعَلُ ٱلْوِلْدَنَ شِيبًا
how will you protect yourself on the day of judgment if you've disbelieved? A day whose terror will be so great that a baby's hair will turn grey..

So he uses the word تتقون to describe protecting yourself... or entering Allahs protection....  that's what the real meaning of تقوی is..... protection.... to protect yourself from Shaitaan... so allah says I gave you fasting so that you can protect yourself...

what does that mean? let's try to understand what that means...

As human beings we all have to go through certain trainings in life... If you want to get good at something.... you have to go through training.... especially physical traininig.... If someone wants to become a soldier or a police officer or a fireman they have to go through specific types of training....  And their body learns to adapt itself.... At the beginning its hard... and then it gets easier and easier and easier for them. right?

And when you're in training things are easy... when you're in a training program things are always easy for you.... for example when they train firemen... they setup a fire.... but they control the fire... It's not like a real fire in the building... So its easier.... They make you jump down a building..... but its easier...  it's not like the real thing.... they go easy on you.... and then when you get good enough they put you in a real life situation... you understand that right?

Now the same thing happens in fasting.... During fasting you're constantly feeling something aren't you? Especially if you're in the US and UK where the fasts are looooooooooooongggg..... you're constantly feeling thirst... you're constantly feeling hunger... there is not a minute that goes by that you're not feeling it.... your throat is fighting with you.... it's yelling at you and saying give me water...  give me water.. give me water... Whether you like it or not your throat is begging you.... and you tell your throat... shut up throat! It's not maghrib yet.....
Your stomach starts yelling at you ...it starts talking to you .... sometimes very loudly especially when others are around.... Come on man! I'm hungry... what's going on? Where's the supply? You're like .... it's fajr dude! you're starting already! You literally have to have a conversation with yourself...

There is a constant war going on inside you when you're fasting... there is a physical war going on inside you when you're fasting.. your throat is against you... your stomach is against you... your body is against you.... your headaches are against you.... Your coffee withdrawl is against you.... Your body is getting weaker and weaker and its begging you... please disobey allah... please disobey allah... and you stay the entire day fasting..... fighting your body and you say... No! My heart is submittied to allah and therefore if my entire body wants something I will not give that to it until Allah allows me to.... you train your heart to control your body..... this is what you do when you fast... this is what I do when I fast.... why is that important?  because when we fast our body grows weaker but our heart grows stronger.... the more our body grows weaker... the more our heart grows stronger... our capital gets fortified.... We draw the power from our body and grant it to our heart.... Why? Because for the country to survive.... the capital and not the cities and villages should be strong!

Because when the fasting is over... when this 30 day of training is over... its time to face the real world.... Now when you step in the world in the remaining months... your heart is ready to control your body.... You capital is completely fortified... All it's defenses are strong.... You are ready to face your greatest enemy.... Who is your greatest enemy?
وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ خُطُوَتِ ٱلشَّيْطَنِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
Do not follow the footsteps of Shaitaan... he is your open enemy
إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَنَ لِلْإِنسَنِ عَدُوٌّۭ مُّبِينٌۭ
Indeed! Without the spec of a doubt... Shaitan is man's greatest enemy

So now your heart and body has undergone this 30 days of training... so once Ramadan is over...  you are not going to eat what your body wants... you will not just go where you want... you will not just look at what you want... because all of those things is what your body wants... but who has gotten stronger with your fasts? Who got weaker and who got stronger with your fasts? the body got weaker and the heart got stronger... and where does taqwa rest?
 فَإِنَّهَا مِن تَقْوَى ٱلْقُلُوبِ
The heart is the place where taqwa lives.... when you give your body the weakeness.. you give your heart the strength to gain control over the body... you train yourself to stop your body from the forbidden things...

So I want to say to your young teenagers especially... don't take this month of fasting lightly.... if you want to enjoy the safety and protection of Islam.... don't take this discipline of fasting lightly....  If you're fasting... and you're staying home all day and just sleeping it off... or watching movies... or passing the day with playstation and xbox.. you are not really fasting.... you're not really fasting... if you're still watching things you should not be watching.... if you're still listening to things you should not be listening to ... if you're still cursing with your tongue and breaking hearts.... you're not fasting... because your heart is still giving in to the wrong temptation... You are not completing the requirements of your training.... The entire traininig of fasting is that you remember that just like I'm fighting my throat... just like I'm fighting my stomach... Just like I'm fighting my thirst... just like I'm fighting my hunger...  I have to fight my eyes... I have to fight my ears... I have to fight my tongue.... I have to fight my desires... I have to fight my hormones....  It's not just eating and drinking.... those are just reminders... those are just symbols... and what is the purpose of a symbol? To remind you of that which it symbolizes.... it is a symbol to much greater things....  the physical restraint....  the physical discipline is a reminder for the spiritual discipline... the spiritual restraint....

And If we don't remember this.... then we become just like the Jews of bani israeel that allah complained about.... they fasted but they didn't have taqwa.... they didn't have taqwa.... even though they fasted! If you and I forget why we were given the fast... then we're doing exactly what bani israeel did.... Allah said to you and me...

كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلصِّيَامُ

علیکم indicates مقدم. this is called maf'ool jar majroor muqaddam... fasts have been made mandatory especially on you.... its your turn now...  I am giving you what I gave them... they did not benefit from fasting .... they did not acquire taqwa... hopefully you won't make the same mistakes they made... hopefully you'll gain taqwa.... that's what allah is saying.... thats what allah is saying when he gave us fasting....

What is the first thing we think of when we are fasting? We think of iftaar! hahah... we think of iftar .... fasting is our training for real life... for the remaining 11 months... and BTW I told you when training goes on... things are easier, aren't they?... controlled fire... controlled falls...  controlled situation... what does allah do for us to make it easier? Doesn't He cage up Shaitaan for us? Doesn't he put the shaitan away? He does doesnt he! he goes easy on us... so that our heart can get a change to train and get stronger... But the second ramadan is over...... Eid mubarak.. guess what? Shaitan is out! and the war begins! and all of that training is now going to come in handy... but if you didn't train your heart properly.... how will you stand up against him?

Because what happens when the enemy takes over the capital our identity? The country is defeated... What happens when Shaitan takes over our hearts, our capital, our identity? We as Muslims... as Momins are defeated....

So we need to take this Ramadan seriously.... because you can complete all 30 fasts and accomplish nothing....
I'll give you an example.... for the young kids... when a guy goes through army training... they make the guy climb over a wall using a rope and go to the other side...Ever seen that?
So two guys join the military..... one guy climbs the wall, the other guy walks around the wall... they both got to the other side of the wall... they were suppposed to do it 30 times... one climbed the wall 10 times... one walked around it 10 times.... they both got to the other side of the wall.... right? now when they are on the battlefield and they have to go over a real wall... whose going to succeed and who's going to fail? the one that climbed the wall will succeeed and the one that walked around it will fail... you understand? They both can say during training I did it! I made it to the finihs line. that's what you wanted, isn't it?Well I did it! Mom/Dad ..... You wanted me to fast 30 days right? Well I did it!

You will fast and I will fast... but my fast will not be the same as yours if I didn't take this training seriously... it won't be the same... because you really applied yourself to this training and I didn't.... I just went through the motions.... we're not going to get the same results.... because what YOU got from you fasts was taqwa.... and all I got from my fasts was hunger and thirst... you understand? In the remaining months... your heart is ready to face the battle against shaitaan ... my heart is not ready at all... I will give in to my first desire... I'll give in to the first temptation shaitan puts before me... you understand?
 

Now let's move on... Allah say in the next ayah... أَيَّامًۭا مَّعْدُودَتٍۢ
The counted days.... this training is for a counted, a calculated number of days... the word used is مَّعْدُودَتٍۢ which comes fromt he word عدد meaning number, or to count... مَّعْدُودَتٍۢ is the past tense.... which means countED, calculatED
Now let me ask you something... you take out 30 bucks from your wallet... you count it... you make me count it and you give it to me for safe keeping... You come back next year and ask me for it... I say I don't remember how much you gave me..... I think you gave you 28 bucks... or I think gave you 29 bucks.... what will you say? You'll say No! I gave you 30 bucks... I say how do you know for sure? What will you say? Because we both counted it.... You cannot change a number that has been counted or calculated in the past correct? Once something is in the past... it is frozen in history... there can not be variations in it... correct?
So these fasts are counted and calculated days أَيَّامًۭا مَّعْدُودَتٍۢ... and the tense used for the calcuation is the past tense... مَّعْدُودَتٍۢ .... so the number of fasts cannot keep changing when they have been counted in the pasts... They cannot be 28 days one year.... 29 days another and 30 days another ... It's a fixed number of days... you understand? 

Let's move on... In the next ayah Allah says...

شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْآنُ هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ

You know when it comes to namaaz, there are many many ayats in the quran..... when it comes to zakaat there are many ayats in the quran... on the subject of taqwa there are many ayats in the quran.... but interesting when it comes to fasting and the month of Ramadan, this is the ONLY ayah in the Quran.... Allah says.... شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ the month of Ramadan... ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْآنُ
Now listen..... what did I tell you ... when people think of Ramdan what comes to their mind? Iftaar, samosey pakorey, fruit chat... You make sure you have enough ketchup in the house.... you go extra shopping for sodas and juices.... Muslims usually don't lose weight in the month of Ramadan.... we gain a few extra pounds in ramadan... that's our practice....
Even if you think beyond the iftaar... what you think of when you hear the month of Ramadan is fasting.... If you don't think of the food... you at least think of fasting ... We think Oh man Ramadan! This year it's coming in the summer.... the fasts are going to be 17/18 hours long.... it's going to be so hard... that's the thought that goes in our head...

Allah says in the Quran....  The very first thing that you should think about when you hear the words .....  شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ The first thought that should go through our mind is...  أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْآنُ  .... not کتب علیکم الصیام  .... but أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْآنُ
the month in which Quran came down..... Ramadan... the month in which the Quran was sent down.... the thing that makes Ramdan special is not the fasting..... the thing that makes Ramadan special is the Quran... that is what Allah is telling us.... That is what Allah is telling us.... now you have to ask yourself..... if you are fasting in Ramdan but you don't recite any quran... you're fasting in ramadan but you've not even memorized a small portion of the Quran .... in the entire 30 days... not even a page... not even half a page.. not even 2 ayats... you didn't memorize  anything from the quran... then how do you understand what Allah is saying? You don't!

The entire purpose of Ramadan is to celebrate the Quran!  the entire purpose of Ramadan is to celebrate the Quran!

Allah made us Muslims a nation.... وَكَذَلِكَ جَعَلْنَكُمْ أُمَّةًۭ وَسَطًۭا I told you the first thing you need for a nation is a capital.... what's our capital? The Ka'aba... the second thing you need for a nation is a constituion... What is our consitution? the Quran... and when the constitution is written..... and passed... then every nation celebrates it... every nation that has a constitution... has a constitution day... the day on which their constitution was implemented... every nation has that day... Our constitution was celebrated... it was revealed in the month of Ramadan... Allah did not just give us one day to celebrate... he gave us 30 days... he gave us 30 days to celebrate the coming of the Quran... This month of Ramadan is celebration of that fact that Allah made us an Ummah..
Now we have our own capital... we have our own constitution and we have our own days of fasting... Nobody else before us had 30 days of fasting... They had a few days of fasting.. less than 10.... But nobody had an entire month to celebrate their constitution.....  The christians did not have شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ... the jews did not have شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ... only us Muslims were given the gift of شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ...  Allah said... now if you want to be called یا ایھا الذین آمنو ... Those of you who believe.... you have to change the direction in which you pray... and you have to change the days you fast... You have to change the direction in which your heart points.... and you have to discipline your body through your heart! Quran is the message for our hearts! It is a reminder for our hearts!
أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَىِٕنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
Only by the remembrance of Allah does the heart find peace... بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ is the key... and Allah called this Quran a Zikr...... إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا ٱلذِّكْرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُۥ لَحَفِظُونَ

This month of Ramadan is a gift for us by Allah.... it is a gift of restrain for our hearts... it is a gift of discipline for our hearts... it is a gift of training for our hearts..... so that we can train our hearts.... so that we can fortify our capital against all enemy attacke....

Not only that Allah made the month of Ramdan in which sins are burnt... they are literally incinerated.....

Ramadan is comes from the word رَمَضَ which means: that which is intensely or vehemently heated by the sun.  And the word الرمضاء means the intense heat of the sun. The Arabs used to say...
 رمضت الغنم  when their sheep got burnt while they were grazing under the scorching heat of the sun, to the extent that their livers became damaged... this month was named so because it burns the sins of the believers. This month trains our heart... makes it strong... makes the desires of the body weak... At the same time we recite the Quran with comprehension and understanding... and the heart starts growing stronger and stronger...... and then Allah burns our sins away.... How does He do that??? A hadees of Rasul Allah (SAS) says
ومن تقرب فی شھر رمضان، بخصلۃ من خصال الخیر، کان کمن ادّا فریضۃ فیما سواہ۔ ومن ادّا فریضۃ، کان کمن ادّی سبعین فریضۃ فیما سواہ

Whoever enters the month of Ramdan and performs a single good deed... he gets the same reward as that of an obligatory deed... of a Fard! and whoever performs an obligatory deed in this month... gets the reward of performing 70 obligations at a different time... And as the quran says...
إِنَّ الْحَسَنَـتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّـيِّئَـتِ
Indeed.... No doubt about it.... Good deeds, eradicate evil deeds...  Therefore Allāh eradicates and removes our sins and wrong doings... by multiplying our good deeds in the month of Ramadan....

Imagine that... a single good deed equivalent to a fard... Waking up and saying Salaam to your parents is equal to performing a fard namaaz... and performing a fard namaaz is equivalent to the reward of 70 fard namaazes.... No it doesn't mean that if you say salaam 5 times ... you can skip your namaaz...

So this month and this Quran is a very special gift given to us by Allah... I want to say this especially to you youngsters in the group, and everybody else in general... you need to make time for the comprehension of the Quran in this month.... because it is the meaning of the words ...  and not just the words that strengthen the heart... I don't care if you do one small surah in the entire month... pick one small surah and understand it.... understand what it means... reflect on it... and then memorize it... You may have your goals... to finish 10 qurans this month... by all means!!!! go ahead!!! I'm not saying no to that... but at least study one surah with underdstanding and memorize it in this month... Because this Quran is a gift....

How would you feel if you gave me something to eat.... You gave me a banana to eat... I take it from your hand and say... Oh thank you so much X and then toss it in the trash can in front of you?? How would that make you feel? Or how about I wrap it up in a nice silk cloth and put it in the top shelf of my cupboard? Won't that surprise you?? You'll say... That's not why I gave you the banana .... I gave it to you so that you can eat... right? What if I peel it ... throw away the banana and start eating the peel? Would you not find that crazy???  But that's what we're doing with the Quran.... The covering or the peel of the banana are the words... the real meaning of the Quran is the actual fruit inside.... We are so happy and so content just eating the peel of the banana.... completely disregarding the actual fruit....  that's not why Allah gave us the Quran? He didn't give us this precious gift... this constitution of our lives.... to just recite it like a parrot... He gave us this gift to understand it... to internalize it... to transform our lives with it... There is no greater gift than the Quran for us! and then he gave us this holy month of ramadan especially to celebrate his gift... now if you and I spend the entire month without reciting one ayah of the Quran... without undersanding one ayah of the Quran... without memorizing one ayah of the Quran... isn't it similar to tossing this gift in the trash can???

And what is the message of Taqwa we discussed earlier? It is taqwa! Taqwa is the key to guidance.... You can't get guidance and then establish Taqwa... you have to first have taqwa and then you get guidance...  ذلک الکتاب لا ریب فیہ ھدی للمتقین. This book is guidance FOR the pious... for the people of taqwa.. So this quran is a guidance for those who have taqwa... Quran is guidance and Ramadan comes each year to create taqwa in us....
يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلصِّيَامُ كَمَا كُتِبَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
Allah gave us fast so that we can have taqwa.... Ramdadan comes to create the connection between us and our creator... it comes to create the state of submission in us which is taqwa... Islam means submission... Ramadan comes to teach us submission... Submission of our will, our desires, our feelings our emotions, our logic , our intellegence to the will of Allah.... Ramadan comes to teach us the most critical lesson that Allah is to be obeyed.... whether we find it easy or not... whether is easy or difficult... we must obey Allah...
Ramdan is a reminder that Allah has a right on us as Maalik (our owner), as Khaliq (our creator), as Haakim (our ruler) as Raaziq (our sustainer) that should be obeyed without question... In Ramadan Allah makes haraam during the day what is otherwise halaal... and we accept this without question and hesitation... we accept it without irriation and protest.... We accept it joyfully to please our Master and to avail his rewards...

May Allah grant me and all of us the desire to read, understand and internalize this precious gift...

And then he says... هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ
Allah says this quran is a guidance for All mankind... This ayah is not just for the Muslims... Allah didn't say
هُدًۭى للعالمین، ھدی للعرب، ھدی لی بنی اسرائیل، ھدی لی بنی اسماعیل
Allah didn't even say
ھدی للمؤمنین، ھدی للمسلمین، ھدی لی جماعت السلیمانیۃ   No no no no no... Allah said هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ A guidance for all mankind...  This is one of the most beautiful things about our religion guys... this is one of the most beautiful things about our religion... It doesn't matter whether you go to our jamaat khana... or you go to a masjid in Austraila, America, England, Pakistan, India... if there are multiple nationalities standing in one row.. you don't make a distinction that the rich people should be in the first row .. middle class should be in the second row... the janitors should be in the last row... No! Everybody stands in the same row.. And it could be...  that the boss is standing next to the worker... and it could be that the worker is in the first row and the boss is in the last row... that could be too... Allah made this guidance for all mankind... you know what that means? He made all human beings equal... Just by the institution of Quran and the institution of Salaah... We cannot be racist.... we cannot be racist because we have namaaz.... we cannot be supremest because we have salaah... salaah is a symbol... a reminder for us that we cannot think that my nation is better than yours... my nationality is better than your nationality.. my skin color is better than your skin color... my language is better than your language...  my religion is better than your religion... my sect is better than your sect... We cannot do this.... We cannot do this... and you know when we are reminded of this? When we are standing together in salaah... when we are standing together in namaaz... When the black skinned, flat footed Hazrat Bilal is standing next to Ali Ibne Abi Talib in prayer... that's when we're remdinded of that... that we are all equal in the eyes of Allah.... that's what هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ means .
You know why I'm highlighting this? Because this has now become very common among us... in Sulemanis.... in our Sulemani Jamaaat..... We think we're better than everyone else... We are superior than everyone else... You know in Pakistan.. there is a word used for sunni and shia... کٹر سنی، کٹر شعیہ... people from India Pakistan have definitely heard this word... it means radical or fanatic sunnis or shias... Today we have become کٹر سلیمانی. we have become کٹر سلیمانی. We look upon Sunnis as they are beneath us... We look at shias as they are beneath ones... we are the ones going to jannah.. and everyone else is going to hell.... Who are we to decide that? Who are we to decide that? Sure we have our aqeeda.. we have our knowledge... and a certain creed based on which we say we are closer to the truth... sure!!! But that should not make us arrogant... that should make us humble... If you and I are born sulemanis..... we should be grateful to Allah for that.... not use that to look down on other sects.... Did Allah not create them with his hands just as he created you and me? Does he not love them as he loves me and you? Does he not feed them as he feeds us?? Do they not recite the same kalma لا الہ الا اللہ that we recite? so then what gives us the right to claim superiority over them?

And by the way being born a sulemani means absolutely nothing.... it gurantees absolutely nothing unless we follow sulemaniyat...  Unless we fulfill the obligations of the Ahad Shareef that we took.... simply being sulemani means absolutely nothing in the eyes of Allah... Because our prophet (SAS) said... خیرکم عند اللہ اتقاکم The best among you in the eyes of Allah..... is the one who protects himself the most.... Taqwa is the key to Allahs grace.... NOT sulemaniyat...   If our beloved messenger (SAS) can turn to his own daughter... to Maulatina Fatimatuz Zehra... the queen and leader of all mominaat... The mother of all Imams... the Mishkaat AlAnwaar...  and say...
یا فاطمۃ بنتِ محمد اِتَّقِ اللہ۔  فأِنِّی لا أَملکُ لَکِ مِنَ اللہ شئیً
Fatima, daughter of Muhammad , you need to have your own taqwa... you need to be cautious and aware of Allah.. because I will have not authority without that, when I stand before Allah in your favor...

When Rasul Allah says that to someone like Maulatina Fatima (صلواۃ اللہ والسلامٌ علیھا).... you think our just being Sulemani without taqwa will help us in anyway on the day of judgment? If we truly believe that we are the right sect... then we should be grateful to Allah for creating us in Sulemani homes... for creating us as Sulemanis...  and we should express that gratitude by fulfilling the obligations of our ahad shareef.... and acquiring taqwa... and we should have love and kindness for those who are behind us.... not look down upon them... doing that makes us less than Muslims....
المسلم اخو المسلم  rasul Allah said... He said a muslim is a brother of another muslim... Whether shia or sunni... they recite the kalma۔۔۔۔ and therefore they are Mulsims and that makes us brothers... How can we make fun of them? How can we look down on them? Wھat's going on here? Don't we make salaah? Do we not do namaaz?  Because if we're not learning from our salaat.. if we are not learning from this Quran then I'm telling you..... we are just a shell.... we are just a shell without any egg inside... that's what our Islam becomes sometimes.......  We look like Mulsims... we speak like Mulsims... but there is no Islam inside our hearts... there is no love for our deen.... looking down upon another muslim brother is not what Islam teaches us... If you are healthy and your brother is disabled... you don't look down upon him... you don't make fun of him.... And if you do.. will that make your parents happy or will that anger them? We are just as much the creation of Allah, as they are!!!! This is not the way of Islam... this is not the way of Sulmemaniyat....

You know when Syedna Hussain bin Ali AsSharfi Qaddas Allah Ruhu... one of our great dai's ... he came to India... And BTW who is more sulemani... you and I or a Dai??? A Dai is way more sulemani than us isn't He? When he was in the jamaat khana... when sulemanis came to greet him and do musafiah.. he would sit and take their musafiha... but when someone from outside... a Shia or a Sunni came to greet him... He would stand up and greet them... Because he said that our brothers are to be honored.....
In the same visit... there was a dinner in his honor... When everybody started eating he saw the driver standing next to the entrance of the masjid... He was wearing the white uniform of a driver.... He asked... who is that man, is he is a Muslim? He didn't ask is he a sulemani? is he a momin? does he believe in Maulana Ali... Does he believe in Syedna Suleman... None of that! He asked is he a Muslim? They said yes he's a sunni... so he asked why isn't he eating? He called the driver with his hand... and seated him next to him in the thaal... and offered him food with his own hands.... This is a true sulemani!!!! Where do we get our creed calling a sunni kafar, a shia kafir??? This is the humility of a true Sulemani!!! Humility should be our symbol... our insignia... not arrogance!

And who greater than Rasul Allah (SAS) فدا امی و ابی.... Has anyone more deserving of pride ever walked this earth? Has anymore of a more elevated status than Him walked this earth??? And Allah prophesizes him in the taurah and zaboor with the sign of humility... He said ... He's soon going to come.... He's soon going to come....
یُریدُ أَنَّ یَبعثُ أُمِیِّ مِنَ الأُمِّیِّن۔وأجعل السكينة لباسه ، والبر شعاره ، والتقوى ضميره ، والحكمة منطِقه ، والصدق والوفاء طبيعته ، والعفو والمعروف خُلقه ، والحق شريعته ، والعدل سيرته ، والهَدِىُ إمامه ، والإسلام ملته ، وأحمد اسمه ، أهدي به بعد الضلالة ، وأعلَمُ بِهِ مِنَ الجَهالة ، وأرفع به بعد الخمالة ، وأعرف به بعد النكرة ، وأكثر به بعد القلة ، وأغني به بعد العيلة ، وأجمع به بعد الفُرقة ، وأُؤلِفُ به بين أممٍ متفرقة ، وقلوبٍ مختلفة ، وأهواء متشَتَّتَة


OMG I don't know from where I remembered this hadeees after so long... This is a quote from one of the previous books.... Allah explains all these beautiful beautiful signs of Rasul Allah prophesizing Him... It's a very long hadees... I just recited a small portion... but after prophesizing all these amazing signs... .... Indeed! I'm sending this man... He will soon be among you.... I will make him the king of all my creations... I'll hand him the keys to my heaven... He will hold my flag on the day of judgment... I will give him the most praised ranks مقام المحمود. I will give him the highest rank in Jannah... No man will be able to enter heaven without his intercession...  No prophet will be able to enter heaven before him ... I will open the doors of my 7 heavens for him...  I will give him the honor of being in my presence and a face to face conversation with me.... I will reveal my presence to him... I will give him the honor of قاب قوسین اوادنی. 

And this man.... My beloved... who will be of such an elevated status.... do you know how he will walk when he's among you? Do you know how he will walk among you? With all these traits... He will live in such a humble manner among you...
لَو یَمشِی عَلی القَصَبِ الیَابِس۔۔۔ لَم یُسمَع مِن تَحتِ قَدَمَیہِ مِن سَکِینَتِہِ

He will walk with such humility... that if he walked on dry leaves.... you would not be able to hear the sound of them cracking under his feet....

This was the seerah of our prophet... By calling sunni and shia kafir... by looking down on them.. which prophet's example are we following?

This Quran binds us together....  هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ
and this was told to the jews... you know why? because the jews believed they were special... they believed they were superior ... everybody else is second class.. we're first class! everybody else is gentiles... we're the chosen nation... just like we say today that we are the chosen sect...  Allah says ANYBODY can become people of Quran... هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ  open invitation to humanity... that completely eradicates racism, nationalism, tribalism, egotism... it gets rids of these things... SubhanAllah... what an amazing religion! We have such an incredible religion!!!

This quran is supposed to unify us... not divide us.... because when we are reminded of this unity when we stand in salaah...
 

هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ

and there are multiple proofs, clear evidences of guidance... meaning the guidance in itself has miracles... that if you study it... you will be convinced that this can only be the word of Allah... وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ
and it distinguishes between right and wrong... All of this up until now ALlah didn't say anything about the Ramadan... Allah started this ayah by talking about Ramdan.. شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ but after that what's the only thing he's talking about? Quran! more than half this ayah is about the Quran... why is he doing that? because he wants to make sure that no muslim ever forgets... that Ramadan is about the Quran ...  Ramadan is about the Quran ... Zahiri Quran as well as the batini Quran....

May Allah grant us all....and me most of all .... the wisdom to understand this beautiful... this miraculous message and help us transform our lives in its light....

I have made these mistakes myself.... I am ashamed to admit it... I have made these mistakes of calling sunni and shia kaafir... I've had very judgmental... very fanatic.. very unforgiving conversations with people... some of them on this group right now.... But I was wrong... I was completely wrong.. May Allah forgive me... But it was through this book that I found that light.... This book can truly change our lives if we approach it with the sincerity it deserves.... And may Allah protect our hearts and by the blessing of this holy month help us fortify it against Shaitaan in the coming months.... may he help us understand the realities behind the symbols he created for us....

آمین یا رب العالمین۔    و آخر الدعوانا عن الحمد للہ رب العالمین۔۔۔۔



























 


So we've been discussing the intellect in the last few sessions... today I want to discuss its counter part... it's partner in crime... the heart... The Arabic word for the heart is قلب.  I first want us to understand the definition of the word قلب and then we'll take a look at its relationship with the intellect and how both are connected together... specifically in the Quranic sense...  But before we discuss that I want to set the stage.....

These next few sessions are going to get quite philosophical.. so young children in the group... you may need to ask your parents to explain things to you after the session... and teenagers and older kids you may need to explain this stuff to your parents after the session..

Volunteer
So imagine you and X are in a tennis tournament together.... You both are competing against each other in this tournament along with many other players... You really want to win this game... You train day and night for it... You change your diet... you change your workouts... you put together a plan and you execute it with discipline... day in and day out ...
X on the other hand, does nothing for the championship... Everytime you see him... he's wolfing down burgers and washing them down with sodas.... The only workout he's doing is with his jaws when he's polishing off those packets of snickers... you understand? Now the day of tournament arrives.... and guess what? For whatever unexplainable reasons.... X wins the championship hands down... He ranks number 1 and you get a lower ranking...
Now tell me.... who won the championship? He did! But who trained really hard for the championship? You did! Who is in an excellent physical condition and who is in a horrible physical condition? But despite that fact who won? X did... Now can you argue that he should not have won because you are in a much better physical condition than him..... What if you go to the judges and tell them how you trained day and night and all X did was eat and sleep... would that make a difference? no!  Why not? You did train hard... You did work hard... he didn't! So then why should you not be declared the winner? Because in the tournament... on the playing field... regardless of everything.... he ranked 1st  ..... You understand? So he gained precedence... even though you had excellence! You agree?
So the lesson we learn here is that in life... as valuable as excellence may be.... as important as excellence may be... precendence trumps excellence!

I'll give you another example... The new iPhone is going to be launched... They are giving one phone away for free at the mall..... The first person to show up at the mall gets the phone... Again you and X want that same phone... X is determined to get the phone... so he makes all the arrangments to get there before you...
